621	pattern, minimality of the resolution is \emph{not} used as input
622	anywhere in the inductive step. Consequently the snake-lemma carving
623	of the minimal resolution (gap (3)) is no longer a substrate
624	requirement: only gap (2) (\(\texttt{pd}\,M > 0 \Rightarrow
625	\texttt{depth}\,M < \texttt{depth}\,R\)) remains the binding gap for
626	the closure of \ref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd}, and the
627	per-syzygy step of gap (1) is consumed only through the SES at
628	\(0 \to \ker f \to R^n \to M \to 0\) (no minimality input required).
629	See \ref{subsec:ab_gap1_haspdlt_pivot} for the SES-descent details.
630	
631	\paragraph{Gap (2) Stacks 00MF proof recipe.} The
632	Buchsbaum--Eisenbud exactness criterion (Stacks 00MF /
633	\texttt{proposition-what-exact}) reads: a complex
634	\(\dots \to R^{r_2} \xrightarrow{\varphi_2} R^{r_1}
635	 \xrightarrow{\varphi_1} R^{r_0}\) of finite free modules over a
636	Noetherian local ring \(R\) is exact at \(R^{r_i}\) (for \(i \geq 1\))
637	if and only if \(\texttt{rank}\,\varphi_{i+1} + \texttt{rank}\,\varphi_i
638	= r_i\) and \(\texttt{depth}\,I(\varphi_i) \geq i\), where
639	\(I(\varphi)\) denotes the ideal of \(r\)-minors of \(\varphi\) for
640	\(r = \texttt{rank}\,\varphi\). The recipe for an iter-201+ prover:
641	(a) define \(I(\varphi) \subseteq R\) as the ideal of all
642	\(\texttt{rank}\,\varphi\)-minors of \(\varphi\) (Mathlib supports
643	this via \(\texttt{Matrix.minors}\) and \(\texttt{Ideal.span}\) over the
644	finite index set of \(\binom{n}{r}\) tuples); (b) prove the depth
645	lower bound via induction on \(i\) using a Koszul-complex argument
646	on the regular sequence implicit in the exactness data; (c) derive
647	\(\texttt{pd}\,M > 0 \Rightarrow \texttt{depth}\,M < \texttt{depth}\,R\)
648	as a corollary by taking the start \(\dots \to R^{r_1} \to R^{r_0} \to
649	M\) of a minimal resolution and applying the criterion at \(i = 1\).
650	Estimated \(\sim 150-200\) LOC project-side; Mathlib upstream PR
651	candidate.
652	
653	\paragraph{Closure assembly (iter-200 ALIGN\_WITH\_MATHLIB pivot).} The
654	iter-200 substrate landing in \ref{subsec:ab_gap1_haspdlt_pivot}
655	replaces the original (1)+(3)-dependent assembly with an SES-descent
656	recipe: given an \(M\) with \(\texttt{pd}_R(M) = k+1\), the
657	per-syzygy step of gap (1)
658	(\ref{lem:exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing}) supplies a
659	surjection \(f : R^n \twoheadrightarrow M\); the SES
660	\(0 \to \ker f \to R^n \to M \to 0\) plus the iter-200 helpers
661	\(\texttt{hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_ker\_of\_surjection}\) +
662	\(\texttt{hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_succ\_of\_hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_ker}\)
663	give \(\texttt{pd}_R(\ker f) = k\) exactly; gap (2) (the
664	Buchsbaum--Eisenbud criterion at the start of a minimal resolution)
665	delivers \(\texttt{depth}\,M < \texttt{depth}\,R\); the iter-200
666	helper
667	\(\texttt{depth\_ker\_ge\_min\_of\_surjection\_finite\_localRing}\)
668	gives \(\texttt{depth}\,\ker f \geq \min(\texttt{depth}\,R,
669	\texttt{depth}\,M + 1) = \texttt{depth}\,M + 1\); a complementary LES
670	upper bound (or the IH applied to \(\ker f\) under the strict
671	descent of \(\texttt{pd}\)) gives the matching upper bound. Combine
672	to get \(\texttt{depth}\,\ker f = \texttt{depth}\,M + 1\), then apply
673	the IH (\(\texttt{pd}\,\ker f = k\)) for
674	\(k + \texttt{depth}\,\ker f = \texttt{depth}\,R\), which rearranges
675	to \((k+1) + \texttt{depth}\,M = \texttt{depth}\,R\) as required.
676	Estimated closure-assembly cost (gaps in hand): \(\sim 50-80\) LOC
677	inductive code.

698	\subsection{Gap (1) first-step substrate (iter-199): per-syzygy
699	  minimal surjection from a finite free module}
700	\label{subsec:ab_gap1_first_step}
701	
702	\begin{lemma}
703	
704	\leanok
705	  [Per-syzygy minimal surjection from \(R^n\) for finite modules
706	    over a local ring]
707	  \label{lem:exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing}
708	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing}
709	  \uses{def:depth}
710	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LK
711	  % (lemma-add-trivial-complex; minimal-resolution carving substrate)
712	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L24590-L24623).
713	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
714	  % "Suppose $R$ is a ring. Let
715	  %     \ldots
716	  %     \xrightarrow{\varphi_{i + 1}}
717	  %     R^{n_i}
718	  %     \xrightarrow{\varphi_i}
719	  %     R^{n_{i-1}}
720	  %     \xrightarrow{\varphi_{i-1}}
721	  %     \ldots
722	  %  be a complex of finite free $R$-modules. Suppose that for some $i$
723	  %  some matrix coefficient of the map $\varphi_i$ is invertible.
724	  %  Then the displayed complex is isomorphic to the direct sum of a
725	  %  complex
726	  %     \ldots \to
727	  %     R^{n_{i + 2}} \xrightarrow{\varphi_{i + 2}}
728	  %     R^{n_{i + 1}} \to
729	  %     R^{n_i - 1} \to
730	  %     R^{n_{i - 1} - 1} \to
731	  %     R^{n_{i - 2}} \xrightarrow{\varphi_{i - 2}}
732	  %     R^{n_{i - 3}} \to
733	  %     \ldots
734	  %  and the complex $\ldots \to 0 \to R \to R \to 0 \to \ldots$ where
735	  %  the map $R \to R$ is the identity map."
736	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LK
737	    (\texttt{lemma-add-trivial-complex}).}
738	  Let \(R\) be a local ring (Noetherian-ness NOT required at this
739	  step), \(\mathfrak m \subseteq R\) the maximal ideal,
740	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak m\) the residue field, and \(M\) a finite
741	  \(R\)-module. Then there exist \(n \in \mathbb N\) and an
742	  \(R\)-linear surjection \(f : R^n \twoheadrightarrow M\) such that:
743	  \begin{enumerate}
744	    \item \(n = \dim_\kappa(\kappa \otimes_R M)\) (the minimal
745	      generator count of \(M\), via Nakayama);
746	    \item \(\ker f \subseteq \mathfrak m \cdot R^n\) (the
747	      ``minimality'' condition: every entry of every relation lives
748	      in the maximal ideal, so the chosen lift has no trivial
749	      summands).
750	  \end{enumerate}
751	\end{lemma}
752	\begin{proof}
753	
754	\leanok
755	  Pick a \(\kappa\)-basis
756	  \(b : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \kappa \otimes_R M\) via
757	  \texttt{Module.finBasis} with
758	  \(n = \dim_\kappa(\kappa \otimes_R M)\). Surjectivity of the
759	  canonical map \(M \twoheadrightarrow \kappa \otimes_R M\)
760	  (composition of \texttt{TensorProduct.mk\_surjective} and
761	  \texttt{Ideal.Quotient.mk\_surjective}) gives lifts
762	  \(m_i \in M\) with \(1 \otimes m_i = b_i\). Define
763	  \(f : R^n \to M\) by \(f x := \sum_i x_i \cdot m_i\) via
764	  \texttt{(Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m}.
765	
766	  Surjectivity: \(\mathrm{range}\, f = \mathrm{span}_R(m_i)\) by
767	  \texttt{Module.Basis.constr\_range}, and
768	  \(\mathrm{span}_R(m_i) = M\) by Nakayama via
769	  \texttt{IsLocalRing.span\_eq\_top\_of\_tmul\_eq\_basis}.
770	
771	  Kernel containment: for \(x \in \ker f\) the relation
772	  \(\sum_i x_i m_i = 0\) tensors to
773	  \(\sum_i (1 \otimes x_i \cdot m_i) = 0\); using
774	  \texttt{TensorProduct.tmul\_smul} and the linear independence of
775	  \(b\) (\texttt{Module.Basis.linearIndependent}) we obtain
776	  \(\mathrm{residue}_R(x_i) = 0\) for every \(i\), i.e.\
777	  \(x_i \in \mathfrak m\). Decomposing
778	  \(x = \sum_i x_i \cdot \mathrm{Pi.single}\,i\,1\) then yields
779	  \(x \in \mathfrak m \cdot R^n\) by
780	  \texttt{Submodule.smul\_mem\_smul} and
781	  \texttt{Submodule.sum\_mem}.
782	\end{proof}
783	
784	This lemma is the \emph{per-syzygy} step of gap (1)'s
785	minimal-resolution carving: given a finite \(R\)-module \(M\)
786	over a local ring \(R\), it produces a minimal surjection
787	\(R^n \twoheadrightarrow M\). Iter-200's ALIGN\_WITH\_MATHLIB pivot
788	(\ref{subsec:ab_gap1_haspdlt_pivot}) replaces the original
789	\(\mathbb N\)-recursive \(\texttt{ChainComplex}\) assembly target
790	with abstract \(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\) SES descent: only
791	the per-syzygy SES is consumed; the iterated chain-complex carrier
792	is not needed.
793	
794	\subsection{Iter-200 ALIGN\_WITH\_MATHLIB pivot: \(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\) SES descent}
795	\label{subsec:ab_gap1_haspdlt_pivot}

833	\leanok

869	\begin{lemma}
870	\leanok
871	[Depth lower bound on the kernel of a finite-free surjection]
872	  \label{lem:depth_ker_ge_min_of_surjection_finite_localRing}

888	\paragraph{Sequencing for closure.} The four iter-200 helpers above
889	plus gap (2) (Stacks 00MF, the \(\texttt{depth}\,M < \texttt{depth}\,R\)
890	input from \texttt{pd}-positivity) suffice for the closure of
891	\ref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd}: the per-syzygy step
892	(\ref{lem:exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing}) supplies \(f\);
893	\ref{lem:hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_projectiveDimension_eq}
894	+ \ref{lem:hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection} +
895	\ref{lem:hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker}
896	give \(\texttt{pd}\,\ker f = k\) exactly;
897	\ref{lem:depth_ker_ge_min_of_surjection_finite_localRing} plus gap (2)
898	plus a complementary LES upper bound (or the IH) give
899	\(\texttt{depth}\,\ker f = \texttt{depth}\,M + 1\); the inductive
900	hypothesis on \(\ker f\) (at pd \(=k\)) plus the rearrangement of
901	\((k + (\texttt{depth}\,M+1)) = \texttt{depth}\,R\) closes. The
902	binding remaining substrate is gap (2) (\(\sim 150\)--\(200\) LOC,
903	Mathlib upstream PR candidate per the recipe in
904	\ref{subsec:succ_pd_gap_sequence}).
905	
906	\paragraph{Iter-201 Path B closure recipe (LES-injectivity +
907	matrix-collapse).} The iter-201 \texttt{ab-stacks00mf} mathlib-
908	analogist verdict identifies an alternative closure that sidesteps
909	gap (2): instead of building Stacks 00MF as a standalone substrate,
910	split \ref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd} into an
911	\emph{inductive step} (\(\texttt{pd}\,M = k+1, k \geq 1\)) and a
912	\emph{base case} (\(\texttt{pd}\,M = 1\)). Each piece uses only
913	already-existing LES infrastructure plus one new
914	\(\sim 50\)--\(80\) LOC matrix-collapse helper.
915	
916	\textit{Inductive step \(\texttt{pd}\,M = k+1, k \geq 1\)
917	\((\sim 50\)--\(80\) LOC).} From
918	\ref{lem:hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection} +
919	the contrapositive of
920	\ref{lem:hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker},
921	\(\texttt{pd}\,\ker f = k\) exactly. Strong induction gives
922	\(k + \texttt{depth}\,\ker f = \texttt{depth}\,R\), hence
923	\(\texttt{depth}\,\ker f < \texttt{depth}\,R\) since \(k \geq 1\).
924	Lower bound \(\texttt{depth}\,M \geq \texttt{depth}\,\ker f - 1\):
925	\(\texttt{depth\_of\_short\_exact}\) part (2). Upper bound
926	\(\texttt{depth}\,M \leq \texttt{depth}\,\ker f - 1\): by
927	contradiction --- assume \(\texttt{depth}\,M \geq \texttt{depth}\,\ker f\),
928	look at the LES of \(\texttt{Ext}^*(\kappa, -)\) at
929	\(i = \texttt{depth}\,\ker f\), infer
930	\(\texttt{Ext}^{\texttt{depth}\,\ker f}(\kappa, R^n) \neq 0\)
931	(forcing \(\texttt{depth}\,R \leq \texttt{depth}\,\ker f\)),
932	contradicting the IH.
933	
934	\textit{Base case \(\texttt{pd}\,M = 1\) \((\sim 80\)--\(120\) LOC).}
935	\(\ker f\) is projective (\(\texttt{pd}\,\ker f = 0\)), hence free
936	over the local Noetherian \(R\) by Mathlib's
937	\(\texttt{Module.free\_of\_flat\_of\_isLocalRing}\) +
938	\(\texttt{Module.Flat.of\_projective}\). So \(\ker f \cong R^m\) with
939	\(m \geq 1\). The inclusion \(\ker f \hookrightarrow R^n\) is an
940	\((n \times m)\)-matrix \(A\) with entries in
941	\(\texttt{Ann}(\kappa) = \mathfrak m\) (from
942	\ref{lem:exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing}'s minimality
943	clause \(\ker f \subseteq \mathfrak m \cdot R^n\)).
944	
945	The \textbf{matrix-collapse helper}
946	(CLOSED iter-201, 4 axiom-clean private decls at
947	\texttt{AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean} L1418--L1517):
948	\[
949	  \forall p \geq 0,\quad \forall e \in \texttt{Ext}^p(\kappa, R^n),\quad
950	  e \circ \texttt{Ext.mk}_0\,(\texttt{ofHom}\,A) = 0
951	  \quad \text{in } \texttt{Ext}^{p+0}(\kappa, R^n)
952	  \text{ when } A_{ij} \in \texttt{Ann}_R\,\kappa.
953	\]
954	The 4 helpers are \(\texttt{Module.elemMap}\) (the elementary map
955	sending \(\texttt{Pi.single}\,j\,1 \mapsto \texttt{Pi.single}\,i\,1\)
956	and other basis vectors to zero), \(\texttt{Module.elemMap\_apply}\),
957	\(\texttt{Module.linearMap\_finFunR\_matrix\_decomp}\)
958	(\(A = \sum_{i,j} A(\texttt{Pi.single}\,j\,1)\,i \cdot
959	   \texttt{elemMap}_{ij}\)), and the closure helper
960	\(\texttt{Module.ext\_comp\_mk}_0\texttt{\_ofHom\_eq\_zero\_of\_entries\_mem\_annihilator}\)
961	itself. The realised API path uses Mathlib's
962	\(\texttt{ModuleCat.hom\_sum}\) + \(\texttt{Ext.mk}_0\texttt{\_sum}\)
963	+ \(\texttt{Ext.comp\_sum}\) and the scalar siblings
964	\(\texttt{ModuleCat.hom\_smul}\) + \(\texttt{Ext.mk}_0\texttt{\_smul}\)
965	+ \(\texttt{Ext.comp\_smul}\) (\emph{not}
966	\(\texttt{Ext.bilinearCompOfLinear}\) as originally projected; the
967	mathematical content is identical). Each summand reduces to a scalar-
968	bilinear form killed by the existing
969	\(\texttt{ext\_smul\_eq\_zero\_of\_mem\_annihilator}\) (L229).
970	
971	With matrix-collapse, the LES at \(i = \texttt{depth}\,R - 1\)
972	gives
973	\[
974	  \texttt{Ext}^{\texttt{depth}\,R - 1}(\kappa, M)
975	  \xrightarrow{\;\delta\;}
976	  \texttt{Ext}^{\texttt{depth}\,R}(\kappa, \ker f)
977	  \xrightarrow{\;(- \circ A)\;}
978	  \texttt{Ext}^{\texttt{depth}\,R}(\kappa, R^n)
979	\]
980	with the second arrow zero. If
981	\(\texttt{depth}\,M \geq \texttt{depth}\,R\), the source is zero,
982	hence \(\texttt{Ext}^{\texttt{depth}\,R}(\kappa, \ker f) = 0\) ---
983	but \(\texttt{Ext}^{\texttt{depth}\,R}(\kappa, \ker f) \simeq
984	\texttt{Ext}^{\texttt{depth}\,R}(\kappa, R^m) \neq 0\)
985	(\(m \geq 1\), \(\texttt{depth}\,R^m = \texttt{depth}\,R\)).
986	Contradiction. So \(\texttt{depth}\,M < \texttt{depth}\,R\), and the
987	base case
988	\(\texttt{pd}\,M + \texttt{depth}\,M = \texttt{depth}\,R\) follows
989	from \(\texttt{depth}\,\ker f = \texttt{depth}\,R\) (since \(\ker f\)
990	free).
991	
992	\textbf{Total Path B cost: \(\sim 130\)--\(200\) LOC} comparable to
993	Path A but with better composition --- every new lemma reuses
994	existing axiom-clean primitives, and the matrix-collapse helper is
995	independently useful for any future ``minimal matrix annihilates
996	\(\texttt{Ext}^*(\kappa, -)\)'' argument. Persistent file:
997	\texttt{analogies/ab-stacks00mf.md} (iter-201).
998	
999	\paragraph{Iter-201 status update: matrix-collapse substrate landed;
1000	closure body deferred to iter-202.} The 4 axiom-clean matrix-collapse
1001	helpers are in place (L1418--L1517, all private under
1002	\(\texttt{RingTheory.Module}\)). What remains (iter-202 dispatch) is
1003	the body of \ref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd} itself:
1004	restructure as \(\texttt{induction } k \texttt{ generalizing } M\),
1005	assemble the base case from matrix-collapse + LES at
1006	\(i = \texttt{depth}\,R - 1\) (\(\sim 50\)--\(80\) LOC of LES
1007	bookkeeping + linear-equivalence transport of matrix-collapse to the
1008	subtype inclusion \(\ker f \hookrightarrow R^n\) through the basis
1009	iso \(R^m \simeq \ker f\)), and assemble the inductive step
1010	\(k \geq 1\) from
1011	\(\texttt{depth\_of\_short\_exact}\) parts (2) and (3) directly ---
1012	\emph{no matrix-collapse needed for the inductive step}, per the
1013	iter-201 prover's discovery (\(\texttt{depth\_of\_short\_exact}\,(3)\)
1014	gives \(\texttt{depth}\,M + 1 \leq \texttt{depth}\,\ker f\) which
1015	combined with IH \(k + \texttt{depth}\,\ker f = \texttt{depth}\,R\)
1016	closes the inductive step in \(\sim 30\)--\(50\) LOC). Total iter-202
1017	closure cost: \(\sim 80\)--\(120\) LOC.
1018	
1019	The iter-202 Lane AB dispatch also discharges the iter-201 plan
1020	agent's option (1) commitment: remove \texttt{private} from
1021	\ref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd} once the body closes,
1022	and additionally promote \(\texttt{isDomain\_of\_regularLocal}\)
1023	(currently private at L2657) and
1024	\(\texttt{regularLocal\_quotient\_isRegularLocal\_of\_notMemSq}\)
1025	(currently private at L2293) to public so that
1026	\texttt{CodimOneExtension.lean} can consume them cross-file as the
1027	project-local Stacks 00NQ witness and the
1028	\(A / (f_1) \to \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}\) preservation helper for
1029	Lane COE Step A1 (see \(\cref{subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200}\)
1030	on the COE side).
1031	
1032	\section{Corollary: regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay}
1033	\label{sec:ab_regular_cm}
1034	
1035	\begin{definition}
1036	
1037	\leanok
1038	  [Cohen--Macaulay local ring]
1039	  \label{def:cohen_macaulay_local}
1040	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay}
1041	  \uses{def:depth}
1042	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM)
1043	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25288-L25299).
1044	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1045	  % "A Noetherian local ring \(R\) is called {\it Cohen-Macaulay}
1046	  %  if it is Cohen-Macaulay as a module over itself."
1047	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM).}
1048	  A Noetherian local ring \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is called \emph{Cohen--Macaulay} if
1049	  \(\text{depth}(R) = \dim(R)\) (equivalently, if \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay as a module
1050	  over itself, where Cohen--Macaulay for a finite \(R\)-module \(M\) means
1051	  \(\dim(\text{Supp}(M)) = \text{depth}(M)\), cf.\ Stacks tag 00N3).
1052	\end{definition}
1053	
1054	% NOTE (iter-185 review, supersedes iter-184): the Lean assembly
1055	% `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` calls a non-private helper
1056	% `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` (AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean
1057	% L1096 — line drifted from iter-184's L944 reference) that now carries a
1058	% structural induction scaffold via `regularLocal_inductive_step`, with the
1059	% load-bearing sorry pushed into two named helpers: (i)
1060	% `exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` consolidating the
1061	% Stacks 00NQ + 00NU substrate gaps in one signature, and (ii) an inline
1062	% technical-bridge sorry inside `regularLocal_inductive_step` for the
1063	% `R⧸(x)`-vs-`QuotSMulTop x R` linear-equiv upgrade (~10-20 LOC iter-186 close
1064	% via `Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul` + `QuotSMulTop.mem_annihilator`).
1065	% Neither has a `\lean{...}` pin in this chapter (the lean-vs-blueprint-checker
1066	% iter185-auslander minor flagged adding a pin for the public-facing
1067	% `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` itself; iter-186+ chapter touch can add
1068	% that hint). The Stacks 00NQ Mathlib gap remains the load-bearing substrate;
1069	% iter-186+ work paths unchanged: (a) formalise Stacks 00NQ in project
1070	% (~300 LOC); (b) Koszul-homology bypass via `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`;
1071	% (c) Mathlib upstream.
1072	\begin{corollary}
1073	
1074	\leanok
1075	  [Regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay]
1076	  \label{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}
1077	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular}
1078	  \uses{def:cohen_macaulay_local, thm:auslander_buchsbaum}
1079	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM)
1080	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25714-L25730).
1081	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1082	  % "Let \(R\) be a regular local ring and let
1083	  %  \(x_1, \ldots, x_d\) be a minimal set of generators
1084	  %  for the maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\). Then
1085	  %  \(x_1, \ldots, x_d\) is a regular sequence, and
1086	  %  each \(R/(x_1, \ldots, x_c)\) is a regular local ring
1087	  %  of dimension \(d - c\). In particular \(R\) is Cohen-Macaulay."
1088	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM).}
1089	  Every regular Noetherian local ring is Cohen--Macaulay: if \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is
1090	  regular local of Krull dimension \(d\), then \(\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)\).
1091	\end{corollary}
1092	
1093	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1094	% "Note that \(R/x_1R\) is a Noetherian local ring of dimension \(\geq d - 1\)
1095	%  by Lemma REF with \(x_2, \ldots, x_d\)
1096	%  generating the maximal ideal. Hence it is a regular local ring by definition.
1097	%  Since \(R\) is a domain by Lemma REF
1098	%  \(x_1\) is a nonzerodivisor."
1099	\begin{proof}
1100	  
1101	  
1102	  
1103	  
1104	  
1105	\leanok
1106	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be regular of dimension \(d\) and pick a minimal generating set
1107	  \(x_1, \dots, x_d\) of \(\mathfrak{m}\). By Stacks tag 00NQ (lemma-regular-domain), \(R\) is
1108	  an integral domain, so \(x_1\) is a non-zero-divisor. The quotient \(R/x_1 R\) is a Noetherian
1109	  local ring of dimension \(\geq d - 1\) (by Krull's principal ideal theorem,
1110	  \texttt{lemma-one-equation}) with maximal ideal generated by \(x_2, \dots, x_d\), hence
1111	  has dimension exactly \(d - 1\) and is again regular of dimension \(d - 1\). Inducting on \(d\),
1112	  \(x_1, \dots, x_d\) is an \(R\)-regular sequence, so
1113	  \(\text{depth}(R) \geq d\). The reverse inequality \(\text{depth}(R) \leq \dim(R) = d\)
1114	  always holds for any nonzero finite module over a Noetherian local ring
1115	  (Stacks tag 00LK, \texttt{lemma-bound-depth}). Thus \(\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)\), i.e.\
1116	  \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay.
1117	
1118	  \emph{Remark on the Auslander--Buchsbaum route.} The same conclusion can be obtained from
1119	  \cref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} as follows: take \(M := R\) itself. Then \(M\) is
1120	  finitely generated and projective (free of rank \(1\)), so \(\text{pd}_R(R) = 0\). The
1121	  formula gives \(\text{depth}(R) = 0 + \text{depth}(R)\), a tautology. The non-tautological
1122	  use of Auslander--Buchsbaum is rather: for a regular local ring of dimension \(d\), every
1123	  finitely generated module \(M\) has \(\text{pd}_R(M) \leq d\) (Stacks tag 00O2,
1124	  \texttt{proposition-finite-gl-dim-regular}); applying the formula to such an \(M\) then
1125	  relates \(\text{depth}(M)\) to \(d - \text{pd}_R(M)\), which is the form most often used in
1126	  practice. The direct regular-sequence argument above is the cleanest proof of
1127	  \(R\) Cohen--Macaulay itself.
1128	\end{proof}
1129	
1130	\section{Application: codim-1 extension on a regular surface (gating A.4.a)}
1131	\label{sec:ab_application_to_a4a}
1132	
1133	The downstream consumer is A.4.a: extending a rational map
1134	\(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) from a regular projective surface \(S\) to an abelian variety \(A\)
1135	across a closed point \(x \in S\) that is missing from the domain of definition \(U \subseteq S\).
1136	The classical mechanism (cf.\ Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, III.7) is:
1137	
1138	\begin{enumerate}
1139	  \item The local ring \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\) is a regular Noetherian local ring of Krull
1140	    dimension \(2\) (because \(S\) is regular and \(\dim S = 2\)).
1141	  \item By Corollary~REF it is Cohen--Macaulay, so
1142	    \(\text{depth}(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}) = 2\). Equivalently, the maximal ideal contains an
1143	    \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\)-regular sequence of length \(2\) (any two transverse generators).
1144	  \item Sections of the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_S\) on \(U = S \setminus \{x\}\) extend
1145	    uniquely across \(x\) because \(H^0_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0\) and
1146	    \(H^1_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0\) (these local-cohomology vanishings are equivalent to depth
1147	    \(\geq 2\) at \(x\) by Stacks tag 0AVF; see Hartshorne, III.3.5, for the surface case).
1148	  \item For a rational map \(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) with codimension-\(\geq 2\) indeterminacy
1149	    locus on a regular surface, the same depth-\(\geq 2\) input produces a sheaf-theoretic
1150	    extension of \(f\) to all of \(S\): pull back any sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_A\)-affine-locally,
1151	    obtain sections over \(U\), and extend across \(x\) by the above.
1152	\end{enumerate}
1153	
1154	So Corollary~REF is the gating input: the geometric A.4.a
1155	proof can be written conditional on the algebraic Auslander--Buchsbaum corollary, and the
1156	two halves develop independently --- as long as Theorem~REF and
1157	Corollary~REF are formalized in
1158	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean} with the signatures pinned
1159	in this chapter, A.4.a can consume them as Mathlib-style imports.
1160	
1161	\section{Lean encoding}
1162	\label{sec:ab_lean_encoding}
1163	
1164	The Lean target file is the new \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean}.
1165	Mathlib at the project's pinned commit (\texttt{b80f227}) already exposes substantial parts
1166	of the depth / projective-dimension API:
1167	\begin{itemize}
1168	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.RegularSequence} provides
1169	    \texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular} (definition of \(M\)-regular sequences),
1170	    matching Definition~REF.
1171	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Depth} (if present at the pinned commit) provides
1172	    \texttt{Module.depth} as the supremum of regular-sequence lengths; the prover
1173	    should re-export this as \texttt{RingTheory.Module.depth} rather than re-define.
1174	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.ProjectiveDimension} (or
1175	    \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ProjectiveResolution}) provides
1176	    \texttt{Module.projectiveDimension} via finite projective resolutions, matching
1177	    Definition~REF.
1178	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.CohenMacaulay} (in some recent revisions) provides
1179	    \texttt{IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing}, matching Definition~REF.
1180	\end{itemize}
1181	% NOTE (iter-184 review): empirical Mathlib b80f227 audit (Lane G task_result)
1182	% confirms FOUR core ingredients of the AB-formula proof are ALL absent:
1183	%   (i)   minimal finite free resolutions (`lemma-add-trivial-complex`);
1184	%   (ii)  the "what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF);
1185	%   (iii) snake-lemma on resolutions (tensoring minimal resolutions by R/xR);
1186	%   (iv)  depth-drops-by-one (Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`).
1187	% Realistic effort estimate: 4--8 iters of substrate work, NOT 30--60 LOC.
1188	% Per the chapter's Application section, the gating consumer for A.4.a is
1189	% Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} (`CohenMacaulay.of_regular`), NOT
1190	% Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} itself — AB formula is NON-BLOCKING for
1191	% A.4.a. The plan agent may safely defer AB formula in favour of closing
1192	% `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` (Stacks 00NQ; the actual `of_regular`
1193	% blocker).
1194	The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula itself (Theorem~REF) is the
1195	content the project must supply unless Mathlib has it at the pinned commit (the Plan Agent
1196	should confirm via \texttt{lean\_local\_search} on \texttt{auslander\_buchsbaum} /
1197	\texttt{Module.auslanderBuchsbaum} / similar). If absent, the file will:
1198	\begin{enumerate}
1199	  \item State Theorem~REF as the formula
1200	    \(\text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}_R(M) = \text{depth}(R)\) under the hypotheses
1201	    ``\((R, \mathfrak{m})\) Noetherian local, \(M\) finitely generated, \(\text{pd}_R(M) < \infty\),
1202	    \(M \neq 0\)''.
1203	  \item Prove it by induction on \(\text{depth}(M)\) following the structure of the
1204	    proof of Theorem~REF above, importing the supporting
1205	    Lemma~REF from Mathlib's depth API or
1206	    proving it in tandem.
1207	  \item Export Corollary~REF as
1208	    \texttt{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of\_regular} (or whatever name Mathlib uses if
1209	    already present) as the consumer-facing statement.
1210	\end{enumerate}
1211	No new core axiom is required; the entire chapter is a homological-algebra construction.
1212	The chapter is independent of the rest of Route A's geometric infrastructure (cube of
1213	abelian varieties, theorem-of-the-square, Albanese universal property), making it a good
1214	parallel-work candidate.
1215	
1216	\section{Out of scope}
1217	\label{sec:ab_out_of_scope}
1218	
1219	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the regular
1220	\(\Rightarrow\) Cohen--Macaulay corollary as required by A.4.a. The following downstream
1221	constructions are explicitly out of scope here:
1222	\begin{itemize}
1223	  \item \textbf{The A.4.a codim-1-to-codim-2 extension proof itself} --- consumes
1224	    Corollary~REF, but the geometric extension argument
1225	    lives in its sibling chapter \texttt{Albanese\_CodimExtension.tex} (planned).
1226	  \item \textbf{Local-cohomology vanishing \(H^i_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0\) for \(i < \text{depth}\)}
1227	    --- a downstream consequence of Stacks tag 0AVF; planned for its own chapter
1228	    \texttt{Albanese\_LocalCohomology.tex} or as part of A.4.a directly.
1229	  \item \textbf{Global Cohen--Macaulay schemes / global depth} --- this chapter only treats
1230	    the local-ring case. The global-to-local reduction is standard but not packaged here.
1231	  \item \textbf{General homological-dimension theory (global dimension, finitistic
1232	    dimension)} --- only the local-ring projective-dimension invariant appears here.
1233	  \item \textbf{Cohen--Macaulay characterisations via \(\omega_R\), dualising complex,
1234	    Gorenstein refinement} --- the formula and its immediate Cohen--Macaulay corollary
1235	    suffice for A.4.a; the duality side is not needed.
1236	\end{itemize}
1237

0	\chapter{Relative Picard sheaf --- \texttt{Scheme.Modules.tensorObj} substrate (A.1.c.SubT)}
1	\label{chap:Picard_TensorObjSubstrate}
2	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/StalkTensor.lean
4	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/Vestigial.lean
5	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean
6	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/PresheafInternalHom.lean
7	
8	\section{Motivation: the abelian-group-valued relative Picard sheaf}
9	\label{sec:tensorobj_motivation}
10	
11	Fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C\) over
12	\(k\). In Kleiman's framework (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor},
13	\cref{def:rel_pic_sharp}), the relative Picard functor
14	\[
15	  \Pic_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat,
16	  \qquad
17	  T \;\longmapsto\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^* \Pic(T),
18	\]
19	is, by definition, abelian-group-valued. The abelian-group structure on each
20	fiber \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) comes from tensor product of line bundles
21	(Stacks tag 01CR / Hartshorne~II.\S 6), and the structure descends to the
22	quotient because \(\pi_T^*\) is a group homomorphism.
23	
24	% SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
25	% Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
26	% abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
27	% groups)
28	% (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
29	% SOURCE QUOTE:
30	% "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
31	%  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
32	%  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
33	% abelian groups defined by the formula
34	% 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
35	% \end{dfn}
36	%  [\dots]
37	% \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
38	%   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
39	% 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
40	%  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
41	% topologies  by
42	% 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
43	% 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
44	%  \end{dfn}"
45	\textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2, Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs.
46	The absolute and relative Picard functors take values in the category of
47	abelian groups; the relative functor is the quotient of one such group by
48	another, and the structure morphisms of the resulting presheaf are group
49	homomorphisms.}
50	
51	Concretely, the abelian-group law on \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the assignment
52	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr] :=
53	\bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
54	with neutral element the class of the structure sheaf
55	\(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) and inverse
56	\(- \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\), where
57	\(\mathcal{L}^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}}(\mathcal{L},
58	\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T})\) is the dual invertible sheaf. Pullback of
59	\(\mathcal{O}\)-modules along \(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) preserves tensor
60	products and the structure sheaf, so the image
61	\(\pi_T^* \Pic(T) \subset \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a subgroup, and the quotient
62	\(\Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)\) inherits a canonical abelian-group
63	structure. This is the content of
64	\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}.
65	
66	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
67	infrastructural, and --- crucially --- much smaller than a full
68	monoidal-category construction. The group law lives on \emph{isomorphism
69	classes} of line bundles: each group axiom is a \emph{proposition} asserting
70	the existence of an isomorphism (\(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\)),
71	and a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it. In
72	particular the coherence data of a monoidal category --- the pentagon,
73	triangle and hexagon identities, and a fortiori any closed structure --- is
74	\emph{never consumed} by a group law on iso-classes. What the law genuinely
75	requires on the carrier is therefore only:
76	\begin{enumerate}
77	  \item a binary tensor-product operation
78	    \(\otimes\, : \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \times \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
79	                  \to \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), well-defined on
80	    isomorphism classes;
81	  \item the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\) as a designated unit object
82	    for \(\otimes\);
83	  \item the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
84	    (\(\exists N,\ M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\)) together with the three
85	    monoidal coherence isomorphisms of \(\otimes_X\): associativity
86	    \((M \otimes N) \otimes P \cong M \otimes (N \otimes P)\), unit
87	    \(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes M \cong M\) (and on the right), and commutativity
88	    \(M \otimes N \cong N \otimes M\). The inverse is carried by the
89	    predicate, not by a separate lemma.
90	\end{enumerate}
91	At Mathlib's pinned commit one might be tempted to obtain (3) by instantiating a
92	\emph{full} symmetric monoidal category on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) and
93	reading the three isomorphisms off it. Two Mathlib facts make such a route
94	conceivable. First, the category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) of presheaves
95	of modules over a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) is already a (symmetric)
96	monoidal category in Mathlib (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\)),
97	with the sectionwise relative tensor \((M \otimes_R N)(U) = M(U) \otimes_{R(U)}
98	N(U)\); this applies verbatim to the \emph{varying} structure sheaf
99	\(R = \mathcal{O}_X\). Second, Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
100	(\(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\)) takes a
101	monoidal category \(C\), a localization functor \(L : C \to D\) at a morphism
102	property \(W\), and an instance \(W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), and produces a
103	\emph{full} monoidal-category structure on the localization \(D\); sheafification
104	of presheaves of modules is the localization \(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the
105	locally-bijective class \(W = J.W\), so a full monoidal structure on
106	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) would follow once \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) were
107	supplied.
108	
109	This full-monoidal-category route is, however, \emph{not} the goal, and is not
110	pursued. The goal is the commutative \emph{group} on locally-trivial iso-classes
111	(à la \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\)), and that group law consumes only propositions, so
112	it never needs a coherent monoidal category --- neither the
113	pentagon/triangle/hexagon identities nor the \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance
114	that the \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) API requires. What it needs is exactly the
115	\emph{existence} of the three coherence isomorphisms of (3), and these are produced
116	directly: the associator by gluing canonical local isomorphisms
117	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, via the single linchpin
118	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), the unitors by the cheap \(\mathtt{mapIso}\)
119	pattern (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}), and the braiding likewise
120	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). With those three existence facts the group law
121	assembles \emph{by hand} the commutative group of \(\otimes\)-invertible
122	\(\otimes\)-iso-classes --- the Picard group
123	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) --- each group-axiom field fed a single
124	existence-of-isomorphism. This is the scheme-theoretic analogue of Mathlib's
125	fixed-ring \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\),
126	whose \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) is read off the \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the coherent
127	monoidal category \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) that
128	Mathlib supplies over a fixed ring; the present construction cannot take that
129	\(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) route, since the coherent monoidal category on
130	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) for the varying structure sheaf is exactly what it
131	avoids. The
132	\(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible},
133	Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) idiom) carries each inverse existentially.
134	The \emph{full} \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) / \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) coherent
135	monoidal category is therefore not built for the group law and is off the critical
136	path; what the group law does still need from this circle of ideas is the
137	\emph{existence} of the associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), realized as a
138	sheafification transport whose single open left-whisker obligation is closed by the
139	stalk--tensor commutation (d.2). The stalk apparatus (d.1/d.2) is accordingly
140	\emph{on} the critical path, while the coherent monoidal category itself is not.
141	
142	\section{Mathlib API survey}
143	\label{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}
144	
145	The relevant pieces of the Mathlib API at the pinned commit
146	(\texttt{b80f227}) were originally assembled into a single \emph{realization route}
147	(route~(e)): obtaining a full monoidal structure on the substrate by instantiating
148	Mathlib's abstract localization-of-monoidal-categories API rather than
149	hand-assembling the associator. That route is \emph{superseded}. The group law on
150	iso-classes never consumes a coherent monoidal category, and the associator is
151	obtained by direct gluing through \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, so the
152	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance on which route~(e) hinges is never required.
153	The survey below is retained as a Mathlib-API reference and to record why the route
154	is vestigial; the four Mathlib facts are all verified against the on-disk Mathlib
155	source.
156	
157	\paragraph{(i) The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category.} Mathlib's
158	\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal} constructs a
159	monoidal-category instance
160	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\) on
161	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,(R \circ \mathtt{forget}_2\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,
162	\mathtt{RingCat})\), with the sectionwise relative tensor
163	\[
164	  (M \otimes_R N)(U) \;\;=\;\; M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)
165	\]
166	% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct` /
167	% `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, in
168	% `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean` (L104, L125)
169	% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean).
170	% SOURCE QUOTE:
171	% "noncomputable instance monoidalCategoryStruct :
172	%     MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
173	%   tensorObj := tensorObj
174	%   whiskerLeft _ _ _ g := tensorHom (𝟙 _) g
175	%   whiskerRight f _ := tensorHom f (𝟙 _)
176	%   ...
177	%   tensorUnit := unit _
178	%   associator M₁ M₂ M₃ := isoMk (fun _ ↦ α_ _ _ _) ...
179	% noncomputable instance monoidalCategory :
180	%     MonoidalCategory (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where ..."
181	\textit{Source: [Mathlib], \texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct} /
182	\texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}, in
183	\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean}, L104--L125.}
184	together with associator, left/right unitors and braiding making it symmetric
185	monoidal. The base presheaf of rings \(R \circ \mathtt{forget}_2\,
186	\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,\mathtt{RingCat}\) is exactly the project's
187	\(\mathtt{Sheaf.val}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) carrier (equal by \(\mathtt{rfl}\)),
188	so this monoidal structure applies verbatim to the \emph{varying} structure sheaf
189	\(\mathcal{O}_X\) --- no project work. This is the presheaf-level analogue of
190	Stacks tag 03DM (relative tensor product of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules).
191	
192	\paragraph{(ii) The localization-of-monoidal-categories API.} Mathlib's
193	\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic} provides the class
194	\(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) and the construction
195	\(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\). The class records exactly

17	decls — re-export is automatic from imports).

25	
26	Downstream files that `import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` see exactly the same
27	surface as before the split.
28	-/

312	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
313	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
314	  \[
315	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
316	  \]
317	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
318	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
319	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
320	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
321	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
322	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
323	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
324	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
325	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
326	  canonical compatibility
327	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
328	     \;\cong\;
329	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
330	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
331	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
332	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
333	
334	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
335	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
336	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
337	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
338	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
339	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
340	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
341	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
342	\end{definition}
343	
344	\begin{lemma}
345	
346	
347	\leanok
348	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
349	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
350	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
351	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
352	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
353	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
354	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
355	  \[
356	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
357	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
358	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
359	  \]
360	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
361	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
362	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
363	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
364	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
365	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
366	  \[
367	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
368	    \qquad
369	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
370	  \]
371	  the associator
372	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
373	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
374	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
375	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they are off the critical path,
376	  recorded in \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
377	\end{lemma}
378	
379	\begin{proof}
380	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
381	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
382	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
383	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
384	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
385	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
386	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
387	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
388	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
389	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
390	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path
391	  (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
392	\end{proof}
393	
394	\begin{remark}
395	[The full monoidal category on all of $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$ is off-path; the group law consumes only propositions]
396	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
397	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
398	        lem:whisker_of_W}
399	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
400	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
401	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
402	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
403	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
404	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. This is explicitly \emph{off-path}
405	  and \emph{not pursued}. One could in principle obtain it as the output of
406	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
407	  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
408	  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), supplying the instance
409	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}); but the group law never
410	  consumes a coherent monoidal category, so this construction is superseded and not
411	  carried out. The substrate provides only the \emph{existence} of the three
412	  coherence isomorphisms that the group law actually consumes.
413	
414	  Were that superseded route pursued, the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\)
415	  would need \emph{no} closed structure: its
416	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
417	  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
418	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} (a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
419	  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
420	  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\)), the same technique Mathlib uses for
421	  fixed-base presheaves in
422	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\). It would use neither
423	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
424	  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (which is
425	  false over a non-affine open and is likewise off the critical path). The \emph{full}
426	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance and the \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) monoidal
427	  category are not on the critical path: the group law is realized without them. The
428	  single left-whisker existence lemma and its underlying d.1/d.2 stalk apparatus, by
429	  contrast, \emph{are} on the critical path, since the associator's sheafification
430	  transport consumes exactly that one whisker obligation
431	  (\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk}, closed via d.2).
432	
433	  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
434	  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
435	  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
436	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
437	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
438	  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
439	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
440	  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
441	  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
442	  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, supplied directly: the associator by gluing canonical
443	  local isomorphisms through the restriction-compatibility isomorphism
444	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), and the
445	  unitors and braiding by the cheap \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern
446	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). The carrier --- iso-classes
447	  of the substrate tensor \(\otimes_X\) --- is the scheme-theoretic analogue of
448	  Mathlib's Picard group \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(
449	  \mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}), but it
450	  is built \emph{differently}: Mathlib reads its \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) off the
451	  \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the coherent monoidal category
452	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) over a fixed ring,
453	  whereas here --- with no coherent monoidal category on
454	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) available for the varying structure sheaf --- the
455	  group is assembled by hand from the existence-of-isomorphism facts.
456	\end{remark}
457	
458	\section{The lift through \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)}
459	\label{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
460	
461	The consumer in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} works not with
462	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
463	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
464	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
465	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section records the
466	\emph{existence-of-isomorphism facts on line bundles} (associativity, unit,
467	commutativity and inverse) that the relative Picard group law consumes. These facts
468	are supplied \emph{directly}: associativity by the direct-gluing associator
469	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (through the linchpin
470	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), unit and commutativity by the cheap
471	\(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}),
472	and the inverse existentially from \(\otimes\)-invertibility. They are \emph{not}
473	read off any \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure: the route that would derive them from
474	\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} (route~(e)) is superseded and off-path. The section also
475	records the closure of \(\otimes\) on the locally-trivial
476	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}) and
477	the interaction with the pullback functor \(\pi_T^*\). The open-immersion
478	restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is on the
479	critical path precisely because it is consumed by the direct-gluing associator
480	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; it is \emph{not} tied to the superseded
481	whiskering-stability lemma \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, which belongs
482	to the off-path route~(e).
483	
484	\begin{lemma}
485	
486	\leanok
487	[Sectionwise lax-monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\)]
488	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}
489	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal}
490	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
491	  % NOTE: this CommRingCat-level lax-monoidal instance
492	  % (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, with helpers
493	  % `restrictScalarsLaxε`, `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) is a self-contained
494	  % supplement. It is NOT used by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, whose proof
495	  % (open-immersion sectionwise base change) identifies restriction along an
496	  % open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
497	  % needs no lax-monoidal pushforward. Retained for potential reuse; off the
498	  % critical path for the tensor group law.
499	  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a morphism of presheaves of \emph{commutative}

8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 1): the bare scheme `ProjectiveLineBar` and its 2-chart cover
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 1** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
13	
14	* the standard ℕ-grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`;
15	* the `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f`;
16	* the projective line `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` / `ProjectiveLineBar` as an object of
17	  `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`;
18	* properness of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` (`projectiveLineBar_isProper`);
19	* the scaffold `GeometricallyIrreducible` / `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instances;
20	* the 2-chart affine open cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`.
21	
22	Downstream strata: `ChartIso` (chart-ring iso), `Points` (k̄-points + `Ga` + `Gm`),
23	`GmScaling` (chart-bridge + `σ_×`).
24	-/
25	
26	set_option autoImplicit false
27	set_option linter.style.setOption false
28	
29	universe u w
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
32	
33	noncomputable section
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
38	
39	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
40	
41	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
42	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
43	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
44	
45	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
46	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
47	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
48	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
49	
50	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
51	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
52	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
53	
54	/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
55	composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
56	`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
57	remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.
58	Required for `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _` to synthesize `CommRing`/`Algebra kbar`. -/
59	noncomputable instance algebraKbarAway (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
60	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :
61	    Algebra kbar
62	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) :=
63	  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
64	
65	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
66	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
67	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
68	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
69	
70	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
71	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
72	irreducibility instances. -/
73	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
74	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
75	
76	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
77	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
78	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
80	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
81	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
82	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
83	
84	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
85	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
86	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
87	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
88	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
89	
90	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
91	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
92	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
93	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
94	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
95	
96	The chain:
97	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
98	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
99	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
100	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
101	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
102	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
103	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
104	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
105	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
106	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
108	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
109	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
110	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
111	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
112	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
113	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
114	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
115	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
116	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
117	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
118	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
119	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
120	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
121	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
122	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
123	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
124	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
125	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
126	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
127	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
128	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
129	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
130	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
131	    · intro x y h
132	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
133	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
134	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
135	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
136	      apply Subtype.ext
137	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
138	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
139	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
140	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
141	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
142	      exact key
143	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
144	  -- proper.
145	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
146	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
147	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
148	    exact hbij
149	  infer_instance
150	
151	/-! ### Project-local Mathlib supplement — MvPolynomial as standard smooth algebra
152	
153	This subsection ships an axiom-clean construction of the canonical submersive
154	presentation of `MvPolynomial ι R` as an `R`-algebra (no relations, generators
155	indexed by `ι`). Mathlib does not ship a direct instance
156	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)`
157	(per iter-182 prover scout); we build it here from the underlying
158	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` machinery. This is a pure
159	`Mathlib`-supplement: it depends only on `Mathlib`, and is independent
160	of the rest of the project.
161	-/
162	
163	/-- The canonical `Generators` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R` indexed by `α`,
164	with `val := MvPolynomial.X` and section `σ' := id`. -/
165	noncomputable def mvPolyGenerators (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (α : Type w) :
166	    Algebra.Generators R (MvPolynomial α R) α :=
167	  Algebra.Generators.ofSurjective MvPolynomial.X <|
168	    fun s => ⟨s, MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left_apply s⟩
169	
170	/-- The canonical `Presentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R` with no relations
171	(`σ = PEmpty`) and generators indexed by `α`. The kernel of the natural
172	`aeval X` map is `⊥` since `aeval X` is the identity. -/
173	noncomputable def mvPolyPresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (α : Type w) :
174	    Algebra.Presentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
175	  __ := mvPolyGenerators R α
176	  relation := PEmpty.elim
177	  span_range_relation_eq_ker := by
178	    simp only [Set.range_eq_empty, Ideal.span_empty]
179	    rw [Algebra.Generators.ker_eq_ker_aeval_val]
180	    change ⊥ = RingHom.ker (MvPolynomial.aeval (R := R) (MvPolynomial.X : α → MvPolynomial α R))
181	    rw [MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left]
182	    ext x
183	    simp [RingHom.mem_ker]
184	
185	/-- The canonical `PreSubmersivePresentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R`.
186	The differential is on the zero module `PEmpty → P.Ring`. -/
187	noncomputable def mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R]
188	    (α : Type w) :
189	    Algebra.PreSubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
190	  __ := mvPolyPresentation R α
191	  map := PEmpty.elim
192	  map_inj := fun a _ _ => PEmpty.elim a
193	
194	/-- The canonical `SubmersivePresentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R`.
195	The Jacobian is the determinant of the zero-sized matrix, which is `1` (a unit). -/
196	noncomputable def mvPolySubmersivePresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R]
197	    (α : Type w) :
198	    Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
199	  __ := mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation R α
200	  jacobian_isUnit := by
201	    rw [Algebra.PreSubmersivePresentation.jacobian_eq_jacobiMatrix_det]
202	    simp [Matrix.det_isEmpty]
203	
204	/-- **Mathlib supplement**: `MvPolynomial (Fin n) R` is `R`-standard smooth of
205	relative dimension `n`. Built from `mvPolySubmersivePresentation` with
206	dimension `Nat.card (Fin n) - Nat.card PEmpty = n`. -/
207	instance mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
208	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ) :
209	    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :=
210	  (mvPolySubmersivePresentation R (Fin n)).isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension <| by
211	    simp [Algebra.Presentation.dimension]
212	
213	/-! ### `GeometricallyIrreducible` instance -/
214	
215	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
216	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
217	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
218	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
219	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
220	  sorry
221	
222	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
223	
224	/-- **Per-chart degree-1 homogeneity witness** for the affine cover of
225	`ProjectiveLineBarScheme` by `D₊(X 0)` and `D₊(X 1)`. Hoisted to top-level
226	out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> simp` proof inside
227	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (per `analogies/gmscaling-cover-bridge.md`
228	Step 1) so the kernel doesn't `whnf` tactic-built proof closures during
229	downstream defeq in `gmScalingP1`. -/
230	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
231	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
232	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈
233	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
234	  fun i => by
235	    fin_cases i <;> simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
236	      MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X]
237	
238	/-- **Positive-degree witness** for the affine cover indexing. Hoisted to
239	top-level out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos` proof
240	inside `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` for the same defeq reasons as
241	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`. -/
242	lemma projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm :
243	    ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i :=
244	  fun i => by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos
245	
246	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
247	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
248	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
249	
250	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
251	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
252	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
253	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
254	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
255	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
256	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
257	    (m := ![1, 1])
258	    (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
259	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar)
260	    projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm
261	    (by
262	      classical
263	      intro p hp
264	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
265	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
266	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
267	        have := hp
268	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
269	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
270	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
271	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
272	        exact this
273	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
274	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
275	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
276	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
277	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
278	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
279	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
280	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
281	        by_contra h
282	        push Not at h
283	        apply hd_ne
284	        ext k
285	        fin_cases k
286	        · simpa using h.1
287	        · simpa using h.2
288	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
289	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
290	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
291	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
292	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
293	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
294	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
295	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
296	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
297	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
298	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
299	
300	-- NOTE iter-197: relocated to AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean per BareScheme smoothness-relocation refactor.
301	
302	end AlgebraicGeometry
303	
304	end
