6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ChartIso
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 3): standard `k̄`-points on `ℙ¹`; `𝔾_a` and `𝔾_m`
11	
12	This file is **Stratum 3** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
13	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
14	
15	* the three standard `k̄`-points `0 = [0 : 1]`, `1 = [1 : 1]`, `∞ = [1 : 0]` of
16	  `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, encoded as sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`;
17	* the additive group `Ga` over `Spec k̄` (= `AffineSpace (Fin 1)`) and its
18	  affine, finite-presentation, and reduced instances;
19	* the multiplicative group `Gm` over `Spec k̄` (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`) and its
20	  affine, finite-presentation, reduced, domain, and irreducible-space instances,
21	  plus the scaffold `GrpObj Gm` and the resulting `Smooth Gm.hom`;
22	* the multiplicative identity `Gm.onePt = η[Gm kbar]`.
23	
24	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`. Downstream stratum: `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
39	
40	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
41	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
42	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
43	
44	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
45	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
46	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
47	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
48	
49	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
50	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
51	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
52	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
53	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
54	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
55	
56	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
57	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
58	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
59	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
60	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
61	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
62	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
63	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
64	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
65	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
66	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
67	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
68	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
69	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
70	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
71	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
72	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
73	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
74	    exact hi.map _
75	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
76	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
77	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
78	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
79	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
80	
81	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
82	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
83	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
84	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
85	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
86	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
87	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
88	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
89	  Over.homMk
90	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
91	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
92	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
93	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
94	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
95	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
96	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
97	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
98	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
99	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
100	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
101	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
102	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
103	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
104	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
105	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
106	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
107	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
108	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
109	      ext r
110	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
111	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
112	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
113	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
114	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
115	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
116	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
117	
118	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
119	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
120	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
121	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
122	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
123	
124	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
125	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
126	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
127	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
128	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
129	
130	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
131	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
132	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
133	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
134	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
135	
136	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
137	
138	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
139	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
140	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
141	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
142	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
143	
144	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
145	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
146	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
147	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
148	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
149	
150	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
151	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
152	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
153	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
154	
155	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
156	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
157	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
158	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
159	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
160	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
161	
162	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
163	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
164	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
165	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
166	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
167	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
168	
169	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
170	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
171	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
172	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
173	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
174	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
175	
176	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
177	
178	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
179	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
180	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
181	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
182	
183	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
184	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
185	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
186	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
187	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
188	
189	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
190	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
191	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
192	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
193	
194	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
195	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
196	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
197	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
198	
199	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
200	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
201	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
202	
203	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
204	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
205	element is finitely presented), bridged through
206	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
207	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
208	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
209	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
210	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
211	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
212	      inferInstance)
213	
214	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
215	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
216	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
217	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
218	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
219	
220	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
221	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
222	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
223	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
224	
225	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
226	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
227	  unfold GmRing
228	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
229	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
230	
231	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
232	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
233	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
234	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
235	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
236	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
237	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
238	  infer_instance
239	
240	/-! ### `gm_grpObj` — `GrpObj` instance on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`
241	
242	The route is per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy (8-step recipe).
243	
244	Step A: build the units-of-global-sections functor `gmHomFunctor : (Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat`,
245	which sends `T ↦ Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents.
246	
247	Step B: build the `RepresentableBy` witness asserting `Hom(-, Gm) ≃ Γ(-, ⊤)ˣ` naturally.
248	The per-`T` bijection is the 3-step chain (over-cat unfold → Γ-Spec adjunction →
249	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` universal property of `k̄[t, t⁻¹]`).
250	
251	Step C: `gm_grpObj := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) gmHomFunctor _`. -/
252	
253	/-- The `(Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u}` functor of units of global sections.
254	This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents. -/
255	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
256	    (Over (Spec (.of kbar)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u} where
257	  obj T := GrpCat.of (Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤))ˣ
258	  map {_ _} φ := GrpCat.ofHom (Units.map (φ.unop.left.appTop).hom.toMonoidHom)
259	  map_id := by
260	    intro T
261	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
262	    apply MonoidHom.ext
263	    intro u
264	    apply Units.ext
265	    change ((𝟙 T.unop : T.unop ⟶ T.unop).left.appTop).hom u.val = u.val

383	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :

74	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
75	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
76	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) →+*
77	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
78	  (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
79	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
80	      (by rw [chartEvalRingHom_X_self]; exact isUnit_one)).comp
81	    (algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
82	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
83	      (Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)))
84	
85	/-- **The base ring map `k̄ →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)`** — the composite
86	`k̄ → 𝒜 0 → Away 𝒜 (X i)` of the algebra map into degree-`0` with `fromZeroRingHom`. -/
87	private noncomputable def kbarToAwayRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
88	    kbar →+*
89	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
90	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
91	  (HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
92	    (Submonoid.powers (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))).comp
93	    (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
94	
95	/-- **The inverse direction of the chart-ring iso**: `k̄[u] →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)` via the
96	universal property of `MvPolynomial Unit`, sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i`. -/
97	noncomputable def mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
98	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
99	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+*
100	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
101	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
102	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i)
103	    (fun _ : Unit =>
104	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
105	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
106	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)))
107	
108	/-- Round-trip on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`: `forward ∘ inverse = id`. -/
109	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
110	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i).comp
111	        (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) =
112	      RingHom.id (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
113	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
114	  · intro r
115	    simp only [RingHom.id_apply, mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway,
116	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C, kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply,
117	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
118	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply]
119	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
120	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
121	        (Localization.mk ((algebraMap kbar
122	            ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0) r : _) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
123	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^0, ⟨0, rfl⟩⟩) =
124	      MvPolynomial.C r
125	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
126	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
127	    simp [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap, chartEvalRingHom]
128	  · intro _
129	    simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply,
130	      mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X',
131	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
132	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply,
133	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
134	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
135	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
136	        (Localization.mk ((MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) :
137	            MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ))
138	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ), ⟨1, rfl⟩⟩) =
139	      MvPolynomial.X ()
140	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
141	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
142	    simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_other, pow_one]
143	
144	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**
145	
146	Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
147	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
148	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
149	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
150	specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.
151	
152	Proof structure (iter-172):
153	1. Apply `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` with `d = 1, v = ![X 0, X 1], dv = ![1, 1]` to
154	   get `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _ | (a, ai) with a₀+a₁=a, ai≤1} = ⊤`.
155	2. Induct on adjoin membership (via `Algebra.adjoin_induction`):
156	   - `mem`: each generator `Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _` equals `isLocalizationElem^k`
157	     where `k = a₀` if i=1 else `a₁`. Hence it's `f (X ()^k)`.
158	   - `algebraMap`: every `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r` is `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away
159	     (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀) = algebraMap kbar Away r₀ = f (C r₀)` since `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)`
160	     is surjective (see `projectiveLineBar_isProper`).
161	   - `add`/`mul`: `f` is a ring hom. -/
162	private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
163	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
164	    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) := by
165	  classical
166	  -- We avoid `set 𝒜 := ...` here because it causes type-class friction with
167	  -- `Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem` and the `SetLike.GradeZero` coercion machinery.
168	  have hfi : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
169	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i
170	  have hgi : (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
171	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
172	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)
173	  -- The 2-generator vector and degrees for `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (d = 1).
174	  let v : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
175	  let dv : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
176	  have hxd : ∀ j, v j ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (dv j) := by
177	    intro j; fin_cases j <;> exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X _ _
178	  -- Step 1: `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) (range v) = ⊤` (i.e. {X 0, X 1} generates `k̄[X 0, X 1]`
179	  -- over `𝒜 0`). We isolate the induction inside a `have` to avoid motive contamination.
180	  have hx : Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0) (Set.range v) = ⊤ := by
181	    apply top_unique
182	    intro p _
183	    refine MvPolynomial.induction_on p ?C ?add ?mulX
184	    · -- C case: MvPolynomial.C r ∈ adjoin via algebraMap_mem.
185	      intro r
186	      have h : (algebraMap ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
187	          (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
188	          ⟨MvPolynomial.C r, MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_C _ _⟩ = MvPolynomial.C r :=
189	        SetLike.GradeZero.algebraMap_apply _ _
190	      rw [← h]
191	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem _ _
192	    · -- add case
193	      intro p₁ p₂ hp₁ hp₂
194	      exact Subalgebra.add_mem _ hp₁ hp₂
195	    · -- mul_X case
196	      intro p₁ j hp₁
197	      refine Subalgebra.mul_mem _ hp₁ (Algebra.subset_adjoin ⟨j, ?_⟩)
198	      fin_cases j <;> simp [v]
199	  -- Step 2: Apply the Mathlib theorem.
200	  have htop := HomogeneousLocalization.Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top hfi (ι' := Fin 2)
201	    v hx dv hxd
202	  -- Key intermediate: surjectivity of `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)` (constants → degree-0 piece).
203	  -- Used for the `algebraMap` case of the adjoin-induction below.
204	  have hkbar_sur : Function.Surjective
205	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar) 0)) := by
206	    rintro ⟨v, hv⟩
207	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
208	    apply Subtype.ext
209	    rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
210	    have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
211	    simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
212	    exact key
213	  -- Helper for the `mem` case: each generator equals `isLocalizationElem^k` for some `k`.
214	  -- The numerator `X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁` of degree `a = a₀ + a₁`, denominator `X i^a`.
215	  -- After simplification: this equals `(X (otherFin i) / X i)^(a_{otherFin i})`
216	  -- where `k = a₁` if `i = 0` and `k = a₀` if `i = 1`.
217	  have gen_eq_pow : ∀ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
218	      (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1)
219	      (hP : (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (a • 1)),
220	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) hP =
221	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi)^(ai (otherFin i)) := by
222	    intro a ai hai _hai_le hP
223	    apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
224	    have ha_eq : ai 0 + ai 1 = a := by
225	      have h := hai
226	      simp only [Fin.sum_univ_two, smul_eq_mul, dv, Matrix.cons_val_zero,
227	        Matrix.cons_val_one] at h
228	      omega
229	    -- Step A: compute LHS `.val` via `Away.val_mk`.
230	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
231	    -- Step B: compute RHS via `val_pow` + `Away.val_mk`. Use explicit `change` to make the
232	    -- isLocalizationElem definitionally visible.
233	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_pow]
234	    change _ = (HomogeneousLocalization.val (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk
235	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi 1
236	        (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) ^ 1) _))^(ai (otherFin i))
237	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_pow]
238	    simp only [pow_one]
239	    -- Now both sides are `Localization.mk`. Reduce via `mk_eq_mk_iff` + `r_iff_exists`.
240	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff, Localization.r_iff_exists]
241	    refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
242	    -- Goal: 1 * (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) * ↑(⟨X i, _⟩^(ai (otherFin i))) =
243	    --       1 * X (otherFin i)^(ai (otherFin i)) * X i^a
244	    -- Use that `↑(⟨X i, _⟩^k) = X i^k` (defeq via `SubmonoidClass.coe_pow` + Subtype.val).
245	    -- Case-split via `Fin.ext + omega` to get clean `0`/`1` for `i`.
246	    have hi_val : i.val = 0 ∨ i.val = 1 := by omega
247	    rcases hi_val with hi | hi
248	    · -- i = 0, otherFin 0 = 1
249	      have heq_i : i = (0 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
250	      subst heq_i
251	      simp only [otherFin_zero, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
252	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
253	      -- Goal: X 0^(ai 1) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 0^a * X 1^(ai 1)
254	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
255	    · -- i = 1, otherFin 1 = 0
256	      have heq_i : i = (1 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
257	      subst heq_i
258	      simp only [otherFin_one, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
259	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
260	      -- Goal: X 1^(ai 0) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 1^a * X 0^(ai 0)
261	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
262	  -- Step 4: surjectivity. Every y is in `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {generators}` = ⊤.
263	  intro y
264	  have hy_in : y ∈ Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
265	      { x | ∃ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
266	          (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1),
267	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a
268	          (∏ j, v j ^ ai j)
269	          (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i) = x } := by
270	    rw [htop]; trivial
271	  refine Algebra.adjoin_induction
272	      (p := fun y _ => y ∈ Set.range (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i))
273	      ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ hy_in
274	  · -- mem case
275	    rintro x ⟨a, ai, hai, hai_le, rfl⟩
276	    have hgen :=
277	      gen_eq_pow a ai hai hai_le (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i)
278	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.X ()^(ai (otherFin i)), ?_⟩
279	    rw [hgen]
280	    have hX : (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.X ()) =
281	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi := by
282	      change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()) = _
283	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
284	    rw [map_pow, hX]
285	  · -- algebraMap case
286	    intro r
287	    -- r : ↥(𝒜 0). Find `r₀ ∈ kbar` with `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀ = r`.
288	    obtain ⟨r₀, hr₀⟩ := hkbar_sur r
289	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.C r₀, ?_⟩
290	    -- Goal: mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r₀) = algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r
291	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.C r₀) = _
292	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
293	    change kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r₀ = _
294	    simp only [kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply]
295	    rw [hr₀]
296	    rfl
297	  · -- add case
298	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
299	    exact ⟨pu + pw, by rw [map_add, hpu, hpw]⟩
300	  · -- mul case
301	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
302	    exact ⟨pu * pw, by rw [map_mul, hpu, hpw]⟩
303	
304	/-- Round-trip on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`: `inverse ∘ forward = id`.
305	
306	Closed by the "cancel surjective" route per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q2: from
307	`mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` (surjectivity of `inverse`) +
308	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right` (`forward ∘ inverse = id` on `MvPoly Unit kbar`),
309	conclude `inverse ∘ forward = id` on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`. The cancellation step itself is
310	mechanical; the only remaining substance is the surjectivity helper above. -/
311	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
312	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
313	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
314	      RingHom.id _ := by
315	  ext x
316	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective kbar i x
317	  -- Goal: ((mvPoly←Away) ∘ (Away→mvPoly)) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = (mvPoly←Away) p
318	  -- The inner `(Away→mvPoly) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = p` by aux_right; the result follows.
319	  have h : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
320	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) p) = p :=
321	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) p
322	  simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply, h]
323	
324	/-- **The chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`** — built from the forward map (via
325	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
326	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
327	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
328	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
329	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
330	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
331	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
332	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
333	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
334	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
335	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
336	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
337	
338	/-- **`kbar`-algebra preservation of `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`** (iter-174 Sub-task A
339	helper per `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3 step 3). The forward map
340	`Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar` carries `algebraMap kbar Away` to
341	`algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` (i.e. `MvPolynomial.C`).
342	
343	Closed via the inverse's `eval₂Hom_C` action: `inverse (C r) = kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r =
344	algebraMap kbar Away r` (the last identity is definitional, by the `algebraKbarAway`
345	`Algebra.compHom` instance combined with `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`). The
346	forward round-trip then gives `forward (algebraMap kbar Away r) = C r`. -/
347	lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap
348	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
349	    ((homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp
350	        (algebraMap kbar
351	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
352	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
353	      algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
354	  ext r
355	  -- `algebraMap kbar Away r = mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r)` since
356	  -- the `algebraKbarAway` instance is `Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0))` and
357	  -- `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away = HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom 𝒜 _` (see
358	  -- `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`), so the composite is exactly `kbarToAwayRingHom`
359	  -- which equals `inverse (C r)` by `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C`.
360	  have hinv : mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) =
361	      algebraMap kbar
362	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
363	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) r := by
364	    unfold mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
365	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
366	    rfl
367	  -- Now apply forward (= homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly) and use aux_right's roundtrip.
368	  have hround : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
369	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)) =
370	      MvPolynomial.C r :=
371	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)
372	  -- Combine: rewrite the inverse C r as algebraMap kbar Away r, then apply roundtrip.
373	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingEquiv.toRingHom_eq_coe,
374	    RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso, RingEquiv.ofRingHom_apply]
375	  rw [← hinv, hround]
376	  rfl
377	
378	/-! ### `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instance via the 2-chart cover -/
379	
380	/-- **Helper for `projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux`**: parameterized by `f := X i`,
381	the chart morphism `Spec(Away 𝒜 f) → Spec k̄` is smooth of relative dimension `1`.
382	
383	Proof: rewrite the composition `awayι ≫ Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar (𝒜 0))`
384	via `awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` to a single `Spec.map (kbarToAwayRingHom)`,
385	then apply `HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` + `RingHom.locally_of` to reduce to
386	`RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 kbarToAwayRingHom`, which equals
387	`algebraMap kbar (Away)`. Close via `of_algEquiv` with `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) kbar` (using
388	`renameEquiv finOneEquiv` + the symm of the chart-ring iso upgraded to a `kbar`-algEquiv). -/
389	private lemma projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_X (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
390	    (i : Fin 2) :
391	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
392	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
393	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos ≫
394	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom) := by
395	  -- Step 1: rewrite the composition as `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom kbarToAwayRingHom)`.
396	  rw [show ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom : ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar ⟶ _)
397	      = Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
398	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
399	          (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))) from rfl]
400	  rw [show (Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
401	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
402	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos ≫
403	        Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
404	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
405	            (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))) =
406	      (Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
407	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
408	            (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos ≫
409	          Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)) ≫
410	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
411	          (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))) from
412	      (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm]
413	  rw [Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp]
414	  -- Step 2: now goal is on `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom kbarToAwayRingHom)`.
415	  rw [HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1)]
416	  apply RingHom.locally_of RingHom.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_respectsIso
417	  -- Step 3: goal is `RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (kbarToAwayRingHom)`,
418	  -- which equals `algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 (X i))` by definitional unfolding of `algebraKbarAway`.
419	  change RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1
420	    (algebraMap kbar (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
421	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)))
422	  rw [RingHom.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_algebraMap]
423	  -- Step 4: build the `kbar`-algebra equivalence `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) kbar ≃ₐ[kbar] Away`
424	  -- and apply `of_algEquiv`. The chart-ring iso `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` ships
425	  -- `Away ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar`; the `_algebraMap` lemma upgrades it to a `kbar`-algEquiv.
426	  -- Compose with `renameEquiv finOneEquiv` to transport across `Fin 1 ≃ Unit`.
427	  let eUnit : HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
428	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃ₐ[kbar] MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
429	    AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv (f := homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i) <| fun r =>
430	      RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap kbar i) r
431	  let eFin : MvPolynomial (Fin 1) kbar ≃ₐ[kbar] MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
432	    MvPolynomial.renameEquiv kbar finOneEquiv
433	  let e : MvPolynomial (Fin 1) kbar ≃ₐ[kbar]
434	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
435	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
436	    eFin.trans eUnit.symm
437	  exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algEquiv (n := 1) e
438	
439	private lemma projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
440	    (i : Fin 2) :
441	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i ≫
442	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom) := by
443	  -- The cover's `.f i` is `Proj.awayι 𝒜 (![X 0, X 1] i) ...`; after `fin_cases i`,
444	  -- `(![X 0, X 1] i)` reduces definitionally to `X i`, and the morphism matches the
445	  -- helper `projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_X`.
446	  simp only [Scheme.AffineOpenCover.openCover_f, projectiveLineBarAffineCover,
447	    Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan]
448	  fin_cases i
449	  · exact projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_X kbar 0
450	  · exact projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_X kbar 1
451	
452	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension `1` over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold.
453	iter-196 substrate landed: the structural reduction via the 2-chart cover is done
454	axiom-clean; the remaining per-chart gap is isolated in
455	`projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux`. -/
456	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
457	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
458	  apply IsZariskiLocalAtSource.of_openCover (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover
459	  exact projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux kbar
460	
461	end AlgebraicGeometry

11	/-!
12	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 4): the `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_×` and product-stability instances
13	
14	This file is **Stratum 4** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
15	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
16	
17	* the chart-bridge `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` (iter-173 `chart-bridge173` recipe step (a));
18	* the per-chart ring maps `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` of the scaling action;
19	* the pullback cover `gmScalingP1_cover` of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`;
20	* the per-chart scheme morphism `gmScalingP1_chart`, the cocycle agreement
21	  `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`, and the over-coherence
22	  `gmScalingP1_over_coherence`;
23	* the bare scaling morphism `gmScalingP1 : ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar`;
24	* the load-bearing fixed-point property `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`;
25	* the product-stability instances on `ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m` exported for Lane B in
26	  `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`.
27	
28	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`, `Points`.
29	-/

42	/-! ### Recipe 1 helpers (iter-184 Lane B, `analogies/gmscaling-projection-idiom.md`):
43	project-side simp helpers for `pullback.map ≫ pullback.fst/snd`.
44	
45	Mathlib's `pullback.lift_fst` / `pullback.lift_snd` are `@[reassoc]` ONLY, not
46	`@[simp]`, so the abbrev `pullback.map _ _ _ _ i₁ i₂ i₃ _ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _`
47	is not collapsed by `simp` even though the equation holds definitionally. These
48	two local simp lemmas unblock projection-chain collapse for
49	`gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` whose STEP 4 uses
50	`asIso (pullback.map …)`. Candidate for upstream Mathlib contribution. -/
51	
52	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
53	lemma pullback_map_fst_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
54	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
55	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
56	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
57	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
58	        Limits.pullback.fst g₁ g₂ =
59	      Limits.pullback.fst f₁ f₂ ≫ i₁ :=
60	  Limits.pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
61	
62	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
63	lemma pullback_map_snd_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
64	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
65	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
66	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
67	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
68	        Limits.pullback.snd g₁ g₂ =
69	      Limits.pullback.snd f₁ f₂ ≫ i₂ :=
70	  Limits.pullback.lift_snd _ _ _

72	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
73	
74	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
75	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
76	definition). -/
77	
78	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
79	homogeneous element `f` of positive degree.
80	
81	Generic in the element `f` AND in the degree `m` so we can apply it to either
82	`(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`,
83	which uses `m = (![1, 1]) i`) or `X i` (which uses `m = 1`). A pure rewrite chasing
84	`awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` + the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
85	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
86	    {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
87	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar m) :
88	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf hm ≫
89	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
90	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
91	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
92	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
93	  rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp,
94	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
95	  rfl
96	
97	/-- **`ℙ¹` is reduced.** Closed axiom-clean iter-168 via `IsReduced.of_openCover` over
98	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover`; each chart `Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X_i))`
99	is a domain because the canonical `val`-injection into `Localization.Away (X_i)` (a
100	localization of `k̄[X_0, X_1]` at a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain) factors through
101	`Function.Injective.isDomain`. Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hP1red`).
102	
103	**Moved to this location iter-193 Lane B**: previously declared after the
104	`gmScalingP1_*` block; relocated so the cocycle proof
105	`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` can pick up the
106	`IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` instance needed by Substrate 1
107	(`IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset`). -/
108	instance projectiveLineBar_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
109	    IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left := by
110	  change IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar)
111	  -- Strategy: `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover`.
112	  -- Each chart is `Spec(.of (Away 𝒜 (X i)))`; `IsReduced (Spec R)` if `R` is reduced.
113	  -- `Away 𝒜 (X i)` is a domain (and hence reduced) because it embeds via `val_injective`
114	  -- into `Localization.Away (X i)`, which is a localization of `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar`
115	  -- (a domain) at a non-zero-divisor — hence a domain.
116	  haveI : ∀ i : Fin 2, IsReduced ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.X i) := by
117	    intro i
118	    change IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
119	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
120	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))))
121	    haveI : IsDomain (Localization.Away ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar),
122	        MvPolynomial.X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
123	      fin_cases i <;>
124	        exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
125	          (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
126	    haveI : IsDomain (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
127	        ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
128	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
129	      refine Function.Injective.isDomain
130	        (algebraMap
131	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
132	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
133	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
134	          (Localization.Away
135	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
136	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))) ?_
137	      intro x y h
138	      exact HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective _ h
139	    infer_instance
140	  exact IsReduced.of_openCover _ (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover

142	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
143	
144	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
145	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
146	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
147	
148	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
149	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
150	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
151	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
152	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
153	
154	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
155	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
156	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
157	
158	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
159	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
160	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
161	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
162	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
163	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
164	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
165	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
166	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
167	
168	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
169	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
170	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
171	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
172	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
173	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
174	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
175	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
176	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
177	        GmRing kbar))
178	
179	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
180	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
181	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
182	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
183	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
184	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
185	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
186	
187	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is
188	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 ((![X 0, X 1]) i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
189	
190	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
191	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the
192	Mathlib precedent `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
193	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`). Used by `gmScalingP1_chart`.
194	
195	**Uniform-in-`i` (iter-179 refactor `cover-bridge-uniform-i`):** the target type carries
196	`((![X 0, X 1]) i)` rather than `MvPolynomial.X i`. The hoisted helpers
197	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`/`_hm` together with the m-generalised
198	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` keep the bridge chain syntactic so `pullbackSpecIso` applies
199	generically — no `match`-on-`i` decoration. -/
200	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
201	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅
202	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
203	        (TensorProduct kbar
204	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
205	            ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
206	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
207	          (GmRing kbar))) :=
208	  pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
209	    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
210	    pullback.congrHom
211	      (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm i)
212	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
213	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)
214	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i))
215	      (show (Gm kbar).hom =
216	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
217	    pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)
218	
219	/-- **The chart-`i` scheme morphism** `(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme`
220	defining `σ_×` on the `i`-th chart. On chart-1 (target `D₊(X 1)`), the affine coord
221	`u = X 0 / X 1` is sent to `u ⊗ λ`; on chart-0 (target `D₊(X 0)`), `t = X 1 / X 0` is sent
222	to `t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The scheme map is built from `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` (the chart-side
223	ring maps) via `pullbackSpecIso` + (the chart-ring iso
224	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar`) + `Proj.awayι`.
225	
226	**Status (iter-173):** body landed via the `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe
227	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). The bridge `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (above) identifies the
228	source with `Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`. The chart-ring iso
229	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` plus a chart-`i`-specific `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` produces
230	the ring map `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗ GmRing`, then `Proj.awayι` lands the
231	result in `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`. -/
232	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
233	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=
234	  (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ≫
235	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
236	      ((MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
237	          (fun _ : Unit =>
238	            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
239	                (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i)
240	                (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar (otherFin i))) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
241	              (match i with
242	               | ⟨0, _⟩ =>
243	                  (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
244	                    (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) : GmRing kbar)
245	               | ⟨1, _⟩ =>
246	                  algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
247	                    (MvPolynomial.X ())))).comp
248	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom)) ≫
249	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
250	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
251	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos
252	
253	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
254	/-- **Shared per-chart helper for `gmScalingP1`** (iter-174 Sub-task A per
255	`analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3). On the `i`-th chart of the
256	`gmScalingP1_cover`, the composition `gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ PLB.hom` agrees with
257	`(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom`.
258	
259	This is the per-chart certificate used by `gmScalingP1_over_coherence` (via
260	`Scheme.Cover.hom_ext` + `Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms_assoc`).
261	
262	**Status (iter-180):** body retired axiom-clean via the empirically-verified
263	`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` recipe of
264	`analogies/pullbackspeciso-bypass.md` (Decision 4). The option collapses the
265	`Algebra.compHom`-driven heartbeat sink on the `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` rewrite
266	that blocked iter-175 through iter-179. -/
267	private lemma gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
268	    gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
269	      (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by

341	The body is a chain of iso steps:
342	1. `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso q awayι_1 (cover.f 0)`: paste the outer pullback
343	   into a left-side composition `pullback (cover.f 0 ≫ q) awayι_1`.

404	/-- **The substantive `(0, 1)` cross case of `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`** (iter-181
405	Lane B helper).
406	
407	This is the *single* non-trivial ring-level identity for the cocycle:
408	
409	`pullback.fst ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 0 =
410	   pullback.snd ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 1`.
411	
412	On the basic-open intersection `D₊(X 0 · X 1) ⊆ ProjectiveLineBarScheme` both chart
413	coordinates are units (chart-0 coord `t = X 1 / X 0`, chart-1 coord `u = X 0 / X 1`,
414	with `t · u = 1`). Under `σ_×([X 0 : X 1], λ) = [λ · X 0 : X 1]` the two chart maps
415	satisfy on the intersection:
416	
417	* chart-0: `t ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹` (i.e. `t/λ`)
418	* chart-1: `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` (i.e. `λ · u`)
419	
420	Substituting `u = 1/t` on the chart-1 side gives `λ · u = λ · (1/t) = λ/t`, and on the
421	chart-0 side `t/λ` corresponds to `u' = 1/(t/λ) = λ/t` under the `u ↔ 1/t` change of
422	coords. Hence the ring-level identity is `λ · u = (1/t) · λ` in
423	`Localization.Away t ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar`, which after multiplying both sides by `t`
424	reduces to `λ · u · t = λ`, true because `u · t = 1`.
425	
426	**Status (iter-182 Lane B):** **structural advance** — the intersection iso
427	`gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` is now built axiom-clean using
428	Mathlib's `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` (per `analogies/intersection-ring-cross01.md`
429	Decision 2). Both sides of the cocycle factor through `awayι_(X 0 * X 1)` once
430	we cancel-epi the iso's inverse; the residual is the ring-level identity above.

432	The `cancel_epi` step lifts the goal from `pullback ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`
433	to `Spec ((Away X_0X_1) ⊗ GmRing) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`. The fully-unfolded form (after

514	  and is retained for potential reuse, but it is \emph{off the critical path}
515	  for the tensor group law. In particular it is \emph{not} an ingredient of

526	  \(f : R \to S\), the module restriction-of-scalars functor
527	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,f\) is lax monoidal
528	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction}, where it is
529	  obtained as the \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) of the
530	  extension/restriction adjunction \(\mathtt{extendRestrictScalarsAdj}\) ---
531	  i.e.\ Mathlib already uses the same mate idiom one level down). The presheaf
532	  monoidal structure is computed sectionwise,
533	  \((M_1 \otimes M_2).\mathrm{obj}\,X = M_1.\mathrm{obj}\,X \otimes M_2.\mathrm{obj}\,X\),
534	  and the presheaf functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) acts
535	  sectionwise as \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{app}\,X)\).
536	  Hence the lax structure maps \(\mu\) (laxity) and \(\varepsilon\) (unit)
537	  assemble from the per-section \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) ones, the required
538	  naturality squares being the naturality of the sectionwise maps against the
539	  presheaf restriction morphisms. Packaging these as a

541	  new diagram chase is needed beyond transporting the sectionwise coherence.
542	  The composite \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) is then lax monoidal by
543	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) together with the monoidality of
544	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) (Mathlib), modulo a definitional
545	  transport across the unfolding of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\).
546	
547	  The commutativity hypothesis is essential: the presheaf-of-modules monoidal
548	  category requires \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of rings, so the
549	  instance must be stated over \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-factored \(R, S\). In
550	  the project this holds: the relevant \(\varphi\) is
551	  \((\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\), which is
552	  structure-sheaf-valued and hence commutative-ring-valued.
553	\end{proof}
554	
555	\begin{lemma}
556	
557	
558	\leanok
559	[Tensor product commutes with restriction along an open immersion]
560	  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
561	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso}
562	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
563	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be
564	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, and let
565	  \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion, with \((-)|_f\) the
566	  restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along \(f\). The canonical comparison
567	  morphism
568	  \[
569	    (M \otimes_X N)\big|_f
570	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\;}\;
571	    M\big|_f \;\otimes_U\; N\big|_f
572	  \]
573	  is an isomorphism. No local-freeness, flatness, or line-bundle hypothesis on
574	  \(M, N\) is required: the statement holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
575	  The reason is structural to open immersions --- restriction along \(f\) is
576	  base change along the structure-sheaf \emph{isomorphism} induced by \(f\),
577	  not along a general ring map --- and base change along a ring isomorphism is
578	  trivially invertible and commutes with tensor products.
579	\end{lemma}
580	
581	\begin{proof}
582	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
583	  Write \(\varphi\) for the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of
584	  rings induced by the open immersion \(f\) (concretely
585	  \(\varphi = (\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\)), so that
586	  restriction along \(f\) is computed by the inverse-image (pullback) functor on
587	  presheaves of modules. The proof exploits the defining feature of an open
588	  immersion: the comparison of structure-sheaf rings over the image is an
589	  \emph{isomorphism}, not merely a ring map. For an open \(V \subseteq U\) the
590	  section ring \(\Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr)\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) over the image
591	  of \(V\) is identified with \(\Gamma(U, V)\) by the canonical \emph{ring
592	  isomorphism}
593	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V : \Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr) \xrightarrow{\sim}
594	  \Gamma(U, V)\)
595	  (the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) isomorphism
596	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appIso}\)). Restriction along \(f\) is
597	  therefore extension of scalars along
598	  this ring isomorphism, and the proof needs no flatness anywhere.
599	
600	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce restriction to the abstract pullback).} The restriction
601	  \((-)|_f\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along the open immersion \(f\) is
602	  naturally isomorphic to the abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback
603	  \((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via the
604	  present comparison \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). It
605	  therefore suffices to produce the comparison and prove it an isomorphism for
606	  the abstract pullback functor.
607	
608	  \emph{Step 2 (move the pullback inside the sheafification).} The substrate
609	  \(\otimes_X\) is sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
610	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}). The pullback commutes with
611	  sheafification: there is a natural isomorphism
612	  \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
613	   \;\cong\;
614	   \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\)
615	  (the present \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\)). Hence the
616	  whole question descends to the presheaf level, where it is the base-change
617	  comparison
618	  \[
619	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(A \otimes B)
620	      \;\longrightarrow\;
621	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
622	      \;\otimes\;
623	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
624	  \]
625	  for the underlying presheaves of modules \(A, B\) of \(M\) and \(N\).
626	
627	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
628	  % \ref{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback} (the pair $(f^*, f_*)$ of
629	  % pullback/pushforward of $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism of ringed
630	  % spaces is an adjoint pair)
631	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L252-273).
632	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
633	  % "\begin{lemma}
634	  % \label{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}
635	  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$
636	  % be a morphism of ringed spaces.
637	  % \begin{enumerate}
638	  % \item The functor
639	  % $f_* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y)$
640	  % is left exact. In fact it commutes with all limits.
641	  % \item The functor
642	  % $f^* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)$
643	  % is right exact. In fact it commutes with all colimits.
644	  %  [\dots]
645	  % \end{enumerate}
646	  % \end{lemma}
647	  %  [\dots]
648	  % Parts (1) and (2) hold because $(f^*, f_*)$ is an adjoint pair
649	  % of functors, see
650	  % Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjoint-pullback-pushforward-modules}"
651	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
652	  \texttt{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}: pullback and pushforward of
653	  $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism form an adjoint pair $(f^*, f_*)$, the
654	  abstract universal property on which the comparison below rests.}
655	
656	  \emph{Step 3 (the genuine residual: H1 alone).} Completing this comparison at the
657	  presheaf level requires a sectionwise handle on the abstract functor
658	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), which is defined as the left
659	  adjoint of pushforward (the presheaf-of-modules avatar of the
660	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) adjunction of the cited Stacks lemma) and admits no
661	  sectionwise formula in Mathlib at the pinned commit. The closure passes through
662	  two presheaf-level ingredients, of which exactly one now remains open.
663	
664	  The first ingredient --- a strong-monoidal upgrade of
665	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the structure-sheaf ring \emph{isomorphism}
666	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\), so that base change along the isomorphism commutes with
667	  \(\otimes\) --- is \emph{closed in this chapter} as
668	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
669	  (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}\)): for a ring isomorphism
670	  \(e : R \simeq S\) the canonical map \(a \otimes_R b \mapsto a \otimes_S b\) is an
671	  \(R\)-linear equivalence \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R
672	  \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M
673	  \otimes_S N)\).
674	
675	  The \emph{sole remaining residual} is therefore the presheaf-level identification
676	  \[
677	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
678	  \]
679	  via \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\). One exhibits a
680	  presheaf-level adjunction
681	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) and concludes
682	  that \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\), being a left adjoint of
683	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), is canonically isomorphic to the abstract left
684	  adjoint \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\). Here \(\beta\) is the structure map of the
685	  restriction functor, so that \((M|_f).\mathrm{val} =
686	  (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M.\mathrm{val}\) definitionally;
687	  composing the resulting comparison with
688	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv} yields the asserted isomorphism.
689	
690	  This presheaf-level adjunction is the presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
691	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\), and must be built from
692	  presheaf-level \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
693	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\). These two building blocks are \emph{Mathlib-absent}
694	  at the pinned commit: only the \emph{sheaf}-level versions exist
695	  (\texttt{Mathlib/.../SheafOfModules/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}), whereas
696	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardId}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) do already exist at the
697	  presheaf level. This residual is being constructed \emph{directly}, mirroring the
698	  existing sheaf-level template app-for-app at the presheaf level (a Mathlib-gradient
699	  build, not a deferral to an upstream PR).
700	
701	  This lemma is the single substrate linchpin of the relative Picard group law for
702	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(M, N\): the associator
703	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} consumes the comparison
704	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_f \cong M|_f \otimes_U N|_f\) on its sole consumer
705	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}, where the restriction is commuted
706	  past the \emph{sheafified} tensor \(M \otimes_X N\) \emph{before} any local
707	  trivialisation of \(M, N\) is introduced --- so the comparison is required for the
708	  global, untrivialised modules, and no free-cover specialisation can stand in for it.
709	  Consequently the H1 residual \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong
710	  \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) is genuinely on the critical path, and closing it is
711	  the \emph{single open substrate obligation} of this chapter. The superseded
712	  route~(e) \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal} \to \mathtt{Sheaf.monoidalCategory}\)
713	  (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}) remains named only as a last-resort fallback should the
714	  presheaf-level adjunction itself bottom out; it is not the plan.
715	\end{proof}
716	
717	\begin{lemma}
718	[Presheaf-level pushforward adjunction and strong-monoidal restriction (H1/H2 substrate)]
719	  \label{lem:presheaf_pushforward_adj_substrate}
720	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans,
721	        PresheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr,
722	        PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj,
723	        PresheafOfModules.isIso_of_isIso_app,
724	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}
725	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
726	  These five presheaf-level declarations are the two ingredients on which the
727	  linchpin \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} rests; both are absent from Mathlib at
728	  the pinned commit and are supplied project-side.
729	
730	  \emph{H1 (the pushforward adjunction).} For a morphism \(\beta\) of presheaves of
731	  rings, \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\) are the
732	  presheaf-level de-sheafifications of Mathlib's sheaf-level
733	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
734	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr}\)
735	  (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}),
736	  reproduced app-for-app one level down at the presheaf of modules. From them
737	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) assembles the presheaf-level adjunction
738	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), the
739	  presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
740	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\). Pairing this against the
741	  existing \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\) through
742	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) yields the canonical isomorphism
743	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) of two left
744	  adjoints of the same pushforward --- exactly the residual identified in Step~3 of
745	  the proof of \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}.
746	
747	  \emph{H2 (strong-monoidal restriction).} \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_isIso\_app}\) is the
748	  sectionwise isomorphism criterion: a morphism of presheaves of modules is an
749	  isomorphism once each of its section maps is. Built on it,
750	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}\) equips the presheaf-level
751	  restriction-of-scalars functor
752	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\alpha\) with a \emph{strong}-monoidal
753	  structure whenever the base-change comparison \(\alpha\) is \emph{sectionwise
754	  bijective} --- the presheaf-level lift of the ModuleCat-level H2 core
755	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}, assembled section-by-section via
756	  \(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal}\). It is to be distinguished from the
757	  module-level, ring-equivalence form
758	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv}\) already pinned in
759	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}: the present declaration is the
760	  \emph{presheaf}-level, \emph{bijective}-comparison form, the one the substrate lift
761	  actually consumes (where the comparison map
762	  \((\alpha.\mathtt{app}\,X).\mathtt{hom}\) is a bijective \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)
763	  morphism but is not presented as a \((\,\cdot\,)\!.\mathtt{toRingHom}\)).
764	
765	  Together H1 and H2 are the two Mathlib-absent ingredients the linchpin needed; they
766	  are self-contained presheaf-level results and are natural upstream-PR candidates.
767	\end{lemma}
768	
769	\begin{lemma}
770	
771	\leanok
772	[Base change along a ring isomorphism commutes with $\otimes$]
773	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
774	  \lean{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}
775	  Let \(e : R \simeq S\) be an isomorphism of commutative rings, and let \(M, N\) be
776	  \(S\)-modules. Restriction of scalars along \(e\) commutes with the tensor
777	  product: the canonical map
778	  \[
779	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N)
780	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
781	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\bigl(M \otimes_S N\bigr),
782	    \qquad a \otimes_R b \;\longmapsto\; a \otimes_S b,
783	  \]
784	  is an \(R\)-linear equivalence. Equivalently, \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\) is
785	  \emph{strong} monoidal, not merely lax, when \(e\) is a ring \emph{isomorphism}:
786	  base change along an isomorphism \(R \simeq S\) identifies the two ground rings, so
787	  the lax structure map of \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\) is invertible. The
788	  forward map is \(R\)-linear, but its inverse \(a \otimes_S b \mapsto a \otimes_R b\)
789	  is only \emph{additive} (it is not \(S\)-linear), so the inverse is constructed as
790	  a homomorphism of additive groups and then verified to be the two-sided inverse of
791	  the forward \(R\)-linear map. This closes the strong-monoidal ingredient of
792	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, leaving the presheaf-level pushforward
793	  adjunction as the sole open residual there.
794	\end{lemma}
795

797	
798	[Strong-monoidal upgrade of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along a ring isomorphism (ModuleCat-level H2 core)]
799	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}

7	\chapter{Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)}
8	\label{chap:Albanese_CodimOneExtension}
9	
10	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-174). This chapter is the A.4.a sub-row of Route A
11	% (the positive-genus arm of \texttt{nonempty\_jacobianWitness}). Per
12	% STRATEGY.md L29 it is the \emph{risk-dominant} A.4 sub-row

14	% (i) \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} --- Milne Theorem~3.1: a rational map
15	%     from a normal variety to a complete variety is defined on the complement
16	%     of a codimension-\(\geq 2\) closed subset. Specialised below to the
17	%     smooth-variety setting where the prover can phrase it as
18	%     "no codim-1 indeterminacy \(\Rightarrow\) extension". This is one of the
19	%     two inputs to Milne Theorem~3.2 in the sibling chapter
20	%     \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}.
21	% (ii) \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} --- Milne Lemma~3.3: for a
22	%     rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
23	%     indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension \(1\). This is
24	%     the second input to Milne Theorem~3.2; it consumes nothing from A.4.b.
25	% A bonus output is \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}: the Weil-divisor
26	% reformulation of (i) that a smooth-surface codim-2 extension is obstructed
27	% exactly by the order of the pulled-back coordinates of \(f\) being negative
28	% at a prime divisor. This is the lemma A.4.a uses to align with the project's
29	% \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor} machinery.
30	%
31	% Depth-\(\geq 2\) extension on a smooth surface, used in the proof body of
32	% \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} when reducing the general-codim case to the
33	% normal-DVR-codim-1 case, consumes
34	% \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} of
35	% \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum} (the A.4.b sub-row). The chapter
36	% records the dependency but does not re-prove it.
37	%
38	% The Lean target file \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean}
39	% does not yet exist; this chapter is the prover-ready specification for
40	% creating it.
41	
42	\section{Setup and motivation}
43	\label{sec:codim1_setup}
44	
45	Fix an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) throughout. By a \emph{variety} over \(\bar k\)
46	we mean a geometrically integral, separated scheme of finite type over \(\bar k\)
47	(Milne's convention, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~v); a variety is \emph{nonsingular}
48	(or \emph{smooth}) if its local ring at every closed point is regular. A variety is
49	\emph{complete} if it is proper over \(\bar k\).
50	
51	A \emph{rational map} \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) between varieties is the equivalence
52	class of a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) defined on a dense open subset
53	\(U \subseteq X\), where two pairs \((U, \varphi_U)\), \((U', \varphi_{U'})\) are equivalent
54	if \(\varphi_U\) and \(\varphi_{U'}\) agree on \(U \cap U'\) (Milne~\S I.3, p.~16). The map \(f\)
55	is \emph{defined at} a point \(x \in X\) if there is some representative \((U, \varphi_U)\)
56	with \(x \in U\); the set of such \(x\) is an open subset \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \subseteq X\),
57	the \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\).
58	
59	The motivating question of this chapter, following Milne~\S I.3, is:
60	\begin{quote}
61	  \emph{When does a rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) extend to a regular
62	  morphism on all of \(X\)?}
63	\end{quote}
64	Without hypotheses on \(X\) or \(Y\) the question has no positive answer (Milne lists three
65	counterexamples on p.~16: the inclusion of a proper open into the ambient variety; the
66	rational map to the cuspidal cubic whose inverse fails to extend; the inverse of a
67	blow-up at a smooth point). The two structural inputs that buy unconditional extension
68	in our setting are:
69	\begin{itemize}
70	  \item \emph{Target completeness:} if \(Y\) is complete (proper over \(\bar k\)), the
71	    valuative criterion of properness forces the rational map to extend across every
72	    codim-\(1\) point of a normal source \(X\) (Milne Theorem~3.1).
73	  \item \emph{Target group structure:} if \(Y\) is a group variety, the difference map
74	    \(\Phi(x, y) := f(x) \cdot f(y)^{-1}\) on \(X \times X\) has a vanishing/pole divisor
75	    along the diagonal that controls the codimension of the indeterminacy locus
76	    (Milne Lemma~3.3).
77	\end{itemize}
78	For \(Y\) an abelian variety both conditions hold, and combining them yields Milne's
79	Theorem~3.2 (the headline of \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}). The
80	present chapter packages the two inputs separately:
81	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension} is Milne~3.1, and
82	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is Milne~3.3. The Weil-divisor reformulation
83	\cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} translates the codim-\(1\) extension obstruction
84	in Milne~3.1's proof into the order-at-a-prime-divisor language pinned in
85	\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}, which is the form the Lean prover consumes when
86	working with the project's \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API.
87	
88	\section{The indeterminacy locus of a rational map}
89	\label{sec:indeterm_locus}
90	
91	\begin{definition}
92	
93	
94	\leanok
95	  [Indeterminacy locus of a rational map]
96	  \label{def:indeterminacy_locus}

99	  % be varieties over \(k\)\ldots" defining the domain of definition of a rational map

1879	This section supplies the one genuinely Mathlib-absent ingredient on which the

1881	of the stalk functor with the relative-ring tensor product of presheaves of modules
1882	(``d.2''). Together with the already-built \(R_x\)-linear stalk comparison
1883	(\cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}, ``d.1'') it closes the single open left-whiskering
1884	obligation for an \emph{arbitrary} whiskering object --- with no flatness and no
1885	local-triviality hypothesis --- which is exactly what makes the sheafification
1886	transport of the presheaf associator work for all modules. The construction is to be
1887	formalized in the dedicated Lean file
1888	\(\mathtt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/StalkTensor.lean}\).

1890	\begin{lemma}
1891	
1892	\leanok
1893	[Stalk of a tensor product (d.2): varying-ring stalk--tensor commutation]
1894	  \label{lem:stalk_tensor_commutation}
1895	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorIso}
1896	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:stalk_linear_map}

1901	  % SOURCE QUOTE:

1911	  % \mathcal{F}_x \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{X, x}} \mathcal{G}_x
