1914	  % \end{lemma}"
1915	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces,
1916	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-stalk-tensor-product}.}
1917	  Let \(R : (\mathtt{Opens}\,X)^{op} \to \mathtt{CommRingCat}\) be the structure
1918	  presheaf of a scheme \(X\), and let \(A, B\) be presheaves of
1919	  \(R\)-modules (objects of \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,(R \circ
1920	  \mathtt{forget_2}\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,\mathtt{RingCat})\)). For each point
1921	  \(x \in X\) there is a canonical isomorphism of \((R \circ
1922	  \mathtt{forget_2}).\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-modules
1923	  \[
1924	    (A \otimes^{p} B).\mathtt{stalk}\,x
1925	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1926	    A.\mathtt{stalk}\,x \;\otimes_{R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x}\; B.\mathtt{stalk}\,x,
1927	  \]
1928	  natural in \(A\) and \(B\), where \(\otimes^{p}\) is the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1929	  product \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\).
1930	\end{lemma}
1931	
1932	\begin{proof}
1933	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:stalk_linear_map}
1934	  \leanok
1935	  The stalk at \(x\) is the filtered colimit over the neighbourhoods \(U \ni x\) of
1936	  the sections functor, and sectionwise the tensor product is computed pointwise:
1937	  \((A \otimes^{p} B)(U) = A(U) \otimes_{R(U)} B(U)\)
1938	  (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj\_obj}\)). The neighbourhood system
1939	  \(\{U \ni x\}\) is cofiltered under inclusion, so the colimit is filtered. The
1940	  isomorphism is assembled in five named stages. Stages (i)--(ii) construct the
1941	  forward additive comparison map and its germ characterisation; stages (iii)--(v)
1942	  upgrade it to an \(R_x\)-linear map, construct the reverse map, and check mutual
1943	  inversion.
1944	
1945	  \emph{(i) Per-neighbourhood balanced bilinear map and its section-level descent}
1946	  (\(\mathtt{stalkTensorBilin}\), \(\mathtt{stalkTensorDescU}\)). For a fixed
1947	  neighbourhood \(U \ni x\) the assignment
1948	  \[
1949	    (a, b) \;\longmapsto\; \mathrm{germ}_x\,a \,\otimes\, \mathrm{germ}_x\,b,
1950	    \qquad a \in A(U),\ b \in B(U),
1951	  \]
1952	  is \(R(U)\)-balanced bilinear into \(A_x \otimes_{R_x} B_x\): it is additive in each
1953	  variable, and for \(r \in R(U)\) the two ways of moving \(r\) across the tensor agree
1954	  after passing to germs, since \(\mathrm{germ}_x(r \cdot a) = (\mathrm{germ}_x r)
1955	  \cdot \mathrm{germ}_x a\) and the stalk tensor is balanced over
1956	  \(R_x = R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\) (the germ--scalar compatibility of
1957	  \cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}). By the universal property of the relative tensor
1958	  product it descends to an additive map on the section module
1959	  \[
1960	    \mathtt{stalkTensorDescU}_U \;\colon\;
1961	      (A \otimes^{p} B)(U) = A(U) \otimes_{R(U)} B(U)
1962	        \;\longrightarrow\; A_x \otimes_{R_x} B_x,
1963	    \qquad a \otimes b \longmapsto \mathrm{germ}_x a \otimes \mathrm{germ}_x b.
1964	  \]
1965	
1966	  \emph{(ii) Colimit descent to the forward comparison map}
1967	  (\(\mathtt{stalkTensorDesc}\), \(\mathtt{stalkTensorDesc\_germ\_tmul}\)). The
1968	  maps \(\mathtt{stalkTensorDescU}_U\) are compatible with the restriction maps of the
1969	  presheaf \(A \otimes^{p} B\) as \(U\) shrinks toward \(x\) (a smaller neighbourhood

5230	\end{itemize}
5231	All \(\uses\) targets resolve either in this chapter, in
5232	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}, or in
5233	\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}. No declaration cross-references a
5234	chapter that does not yet exist on disk.
5235	
5236	\section{Sheaf internal-hom of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (the \(\otimes\)-inverse
5237	  infrastructure)}
5238	\label{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}
5239	
5240	The PRIMARY group law (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) needs a
5241	\(\otimes\)-inverse for every line bundle, supplied by
5242	\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} as the dual
5243	\(L^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)\). The standard route
5244	constructs that dual as a special case of the \emph{sheaf internal hom} of
5245	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules; over a fixed ring the internal hom of \(R\)-modules is the
5246	linear-coyoneda module \(\mathtt{ihom}\,M\,N = (M \to N)\), with dual
5247	\(\mathtt{ihom}\,M\,\mathbf{1} = M^\vee\), but for the \emph{varying} structure sheaf
5248	no such
5249	internal-hom / monoidal-closed structure exists at the
5250	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\), \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\), or general-categorical
5251	level. This section decomposes the construction of the missing object into
5252	individually-formalizable sub-steps, following the slice presentation of the internal
5253	hom; each block names the intended Lean declaration and its dependencies. The
5254	specialisation that closes \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} is recorded in
5255	\cref{rem:dual_discharges_inverse}; an alternative descent-theoretic route is noted in
5256	\cref{rem:dual_via_stack}.
5257	
5258	\emph{Status: deferred, off the group's critical path.} The blocks of this section
5259	--- the dual object \(\mathtt{dual}\) and its restriction compatibility
5260	\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}, the local-triviality of the dual
5261	\cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}, the gluing engine
5262	\cref{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}, and the discharge remark
5263	\cref{rem:dual_discharges_inverse} --- together constitute the \emph{deferred}
5264	\(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Leftrightarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) bridge: on a
5265	scheme an invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is locally free of rank one, and
5266	conversely (Stacks tag 0B8M), so the two carriers agree, and the locally-trivial
5267	carrier's missing inverse (\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}) is recovered
5268	precisely from the dual built here.
5269	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 0B8M (Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Invertible
5270	% modules), Lemma lemma-invertible-is-locally-free-rank-1
5271	% (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4159-L4165).
5272	% SOURCE QUOTE:
5273	% "\begin{lemma}
5274	% \label{lemma-invertible-is-locally-free-rank-1}
5275	% Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. Any locally free
5276	% $\mathcal{O}_X$-module of rank $1$ is invertible.
5277	% If all stalks $\mathcal{O}_{X, x}$ are local rings, then
5278	% the converse holds as well (but in general this is not the case).
5279	% \end{lemma}"
5280	However, the relative Picard \emph{group law} is now carried on the
5281	\(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
5282	(\cref{sec:tensorobj_pic_carrier}, \cref{thm:pic_commgroup}), where the inverse is
5283	the membership witness and \emph{no} dual object is constructed. This section is
5284	therefore needed only by a downstream consumer that specifically requires the
5285	locally-trivial form of an inverse --- not by the group law. The blocks are retained
5286	intact for that future use.
5287	
5288	A structural point governs the whole construction. The naive rule
5289	\(U \mapsto \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(M(U), N(U))\) is \emph{contravariant} in the
5290	restriction maps of \(M\) (a restriction \(M(U) \to M(V)\) for \(V \subseteq U\)
5291	induces precomposition \(\Hom(M(V), N(V)) \to \Hom(M(U), \cdot)\) in the wrong
5292	direction), so it is \emph{not} a presheaf in the covariant sense and cannot be
5293	sheafified like the tensor product \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}. The remedy is
5294	the \emph{slice} formula \(U \mapsto (M|_U \to N|_U)\) --- the module of morphisms of
5295	\emph{restricted} modules over the restricted ring --- whose presheaf functoriality
5296	(restrict a global-over-\(U\) morphism to a global-over-\(V\) morphism, for
5297	\(V \subseteq U\)) \emph{is} covariant. Constructing that slice presheaf, and
5298	proving it satisfies the sheaf condition, is the substance of the build.
5299	
5300	\begin{definition}
5301	
5302	\leanok
5303	[The global-ring module on morphisms of presheaves of modules (value module)]
5304	  \label{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value}
5305	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.homModule}
5306	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
5307	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Modules on Ringed Spaces", \S Internal Hom
5308	  % (\texttt{section-internal-hom}, tag area 01CM)
5309	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L3508--L3514).
5310	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
5311	  % "abelian groups. In addition, given an element
5312	  % $\varphi \in \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X|_U}(\mathcal{F}|_U, \mathcal{G}|_U)$
5313	  % and a section $f \in \mathcal{O}_X(U)$ then we can define
5314	  % $f\varphi \in \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X|_U}(\mathcal{F}|_U, \mathcal{G}|_U)$
5315	  % by either precomposing with multiplication by $f$ on $\mathcal{F}|_U$
5316	  % or postcomposing with multiplication by $f$ on $\mathcal{G}|_U$ (it gives
5317	  % the same result). Hence we in fact get a sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules."
5318	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], ``Modules on Ringed Spaces'', \S Internal Hom
5319	  (tag area 01CM): the section ring acts on a morphism of restricted modules by
5320	  post-composition with multiplication by the section.}
5321	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a base category \(\mathcal{C}\)
5322	  that has a terminal object \(T\), and let \(M, N \in \mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\).
5323	  The abelian group of morphisms \(M \longrightarrow N\) of presheaves of \(R\)-modules
5324	  carries a canonical \(R(T)\)-module structure --- the \emph{morphism module}
5325	  \(\mathtt{homModule}\,M\,N\). A global scalar \(f \in R(T)\) acts on a morphism
5326	  \(\varphi : M \to N\) by post-composition with multiplication by \(f\),
5327	  \[
5328	    f \cdot \varphi \;:=\; m_f^{N} \circ \varphi,
5329	  \]
5330	  where \(m_f^{N} : N \to N\) is the \emph{multiplication-by-\(f\)} endomorphism of
5331	  \(N\): the endomorphism that, on each object \(U \in \mathcal{C}\), is multiplication
5332	  by the restriction \(f|_U \in R(U)\) of \(f\) along the unique morphism \(U \to T\)
5333	  to the terminal object. The module axioms hold because the assignment
5334	  \(f \mapsto m_f^{N}\) is a ring homomorphism \(R(T) \to \mathrm{End}(N)\) into the
5335	  endomorphism ring of \(N\) --- a product \(f \cdot g\) goes to the \emph{composite}
5336	  \(m_f^{N} \circ m_g^{N}\) of the two multiplication endomorphisms, the order in which
5337	  the factors compose being reversed by the fact that the scalar acts by
5338	  \emph{post}-composition --- and composition of morphisms is biadditive in the
5339	  preadditive category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\). Distributivity and the
5340	  associativity of the action then follow from bilinearity of composition together with
5341	  the homomorphism property of \(f \mapsto m_f^{N}\). This is the genuinely new core of
5342	  the construction: Mathlib supplies the \emph{fixed}-ring internal hom
5343	  \(\mathtt{ihom}\,M\,N = (M \to N)\) over a single base ring, but nothing equipping the
5344	  morphism group with the module structure for the \emph{varying} structure presheaf at
5345	  the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) level.
5346	\end{definition}
5347	
5348	\begin{definition}
5349	
5350	\leanok
5351	[Slice specialisation of the morphism module]
5352	  \label{def:presheaf_internal_hom_slice_value}
5353	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.internalHomObjModule}
5354	  \uses{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value}
5355	  Specialise \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value} to a slice category. For an object
5356	  (open) \(U\) of \(\mathcal{C} = \Opens X\), restricting along the forgetful functor
5357	  \(\mathtt{Over.forget}\,U : \mathcal{C}/U \to \mathcal{C}\) --- the open-immersion
5358	  restriction \(M \mapsto M|_U\) --- carries \(M, N\) to presheaves of modules
5359	  \(M|_U, N|_U\) over the restricted structure ring on the slice \(\mathcal{C}/U\). The
5360	  slice \(\mathcal{C}/U\) has the terminal object \(\mathtt{Over.mk}\,(\mathrm{id}_U)\),
5361	  at which the restricted ring takes the value \(R(U)\); hence
5362	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value} makes the morphism group
5363	  \(M|_U \longrightarrow N|_U\) an \(R(U)\)-module, the
5364	  \(\mathtt{internalHomObjModule}\). This \(R(U)\)-module \((M|_U \to N|_U)\) is exactly
5365	  the value \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)(U)\) of \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom}.
5366	\end{definition}
5367	
5368	\begin{definition}
5369	
5370	\leanok
5371	[Presheaf internal hom of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules]
5372	  \label{def:presheaf_internal_hom}
5373	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.internalHom}
5374	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:presheaf_internal_hom_value,
5375	        def:presheaf_internal_hom_slice_value, lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction}
5376	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Modules on Ringed Spaces", \S Internal Hom
5377	  % (\texttt{section-internal-hom}, tag area 01CM)
5378	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L3500--L3524).
5379	  % SOURCE QUOTE:

6	% \(\mathbb{G}_m\)-scaling cocycle (path (III.c) of
7	% chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity) together with two off-target sorries

9	% \texttt{Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean}. Both substrates are
10	% generic in the geometric situation (the closed-immersion lift lemma is
11	% scheme-level; the tensor-domain lemma is ring-level keyed on the
12	% \texttt{projectiveLineBar} grading) and live in their own file rather
13	% than inline so they can be re-used by the three GmScaling
14	% applications and, if upstreamed, by Mathlib itself.
15	%
16	% The recipe consolidated below was produced by the
17	% \texttt{mathlib-analogist} subagent in iter-189 and is preserved
18	% verbatim in \texttt{analogies/lane-b-substrate.md} (Section
19	% ``Concrete project-side recipe''). The chapter itself is the

22	\section{Setup and motivation}
23	\label{sec:cross01_substrate_setup}
24	
25	The Lane B cocycle \texttt{gmScalingP1\_chart\_agreement\_cross01} reduces, after
26	the iter-188 (III.c) separated-locus structural setup, to a single substantive
27	factorisation: a pair morphism
28	\(s_{\text{pair}} : T \to T_0 \times_X T_1\) into a fibre product over the
29	projective line bar \(\mathrm{PLB}_{\overline{k}}\) must factor through the
30	diagonal closed immersion
31	\(\Delta : T_0 \times_{X} T_1 \hookrightarrow T_0 \times_{\overline{k}} T_1\)
32	of the separated-over-\(\overline{k}\) target. Concretely the missing primitive is
33	the equivalence
34	\[
35	  \exists\, h : Y \to Z,\ h \circ i = g \iff \mathrm{range}(g) \subseteq \mathrm{range}(i)
36	\]
37	for a closed immersion \(i\) with reduced source/target hypothesis, which Mathlib
38	ships only in the kernel-inequality form (\(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift}\),
39	keyed on \(f.\mathrm{ker} \le g.\mathrm{ker}\)). Substrate~1 fills this gap.
40	
41	The same Lane B cocycle, together with the two off-target instances
42	\texttt{gm\_geomIrred} and \texttt{projGm\_isReduced}, also requires
43	\[

45	\]
46	for a homogeneous generator \(f\) of positive degree in the

48	canonical iso chain

\chapter{Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form; Mumford \S4 in the abelian-variety case)}
\label{chap:Rigidity}

This chapter records a self-contained helper: \emph{rigidity for morphisms of schemes that agree on a non-empty open}. The classical motivation is Mumford's rigidity for abelian-variety morphisms, but the project's formalised statement is more general: the source-side group-object hypothesis is unused by the proof and is dropped.

The lemma is independent of the construction of \(\Jac(C)\). It is the load-bearing input for the M2.a sub-step of the genus-0 Albanese-witness argument (Jacobian.tex \S C.2.b), where the source is taken to be \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) (which is \emph{not} a group scheme).

# Blueprint Review Report

## Slug
iter272

272

---

## Top-level summaries

### Incomplete parts

- `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`: 123 lean-aux nodes across 5 covered sub-files lack blueprint entries (40 from `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`, 32 from `PresheafInternalHom.lean`, 24 from `StalkTensor.lean`, 14 from `DualInverse.lean`, 13 from `Vestigial.lean`). The main public API is covered but intermediate public lemmas in the PresheafInternalHom and StalkTensor clusters are not individually blueprinted.
- `Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex`: 31 lean-aux nodes unmatched, including several non-private intermediate helpers from the Path B matrix-collapse chain (L1418–L1517 private helpers, but also the `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_*` public lemma family).
- `Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`: 30 lean-aux nodes unmatched; `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` has `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.weilDivisorObstruction}` (unformalised stub); and Route-C dependency violation (see below).
- `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex`: 16 lean-aux nodes unmatched; 9 blueprint nodes have `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.*}` stubs (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, `lem:flat_locus_open`, `lem:nonflat_locus_proper`, `lem:noetherian_induction_strata`, `lem:smooth_proper_curve_projective`, `lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W`, `lem:quot_boundedness`, `lem:quot_alpha_injective`, `lem:quot_valuative_criterion`).
- Genus0BaseObjects sub-files: 5 files (`BareScheme.lean`, `ChartIso.lean`, `Points.lean`, `GmScaling.lean`, root `Genus0BaseObjects.lean`) have no dedicated chapter. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` partially covers them via `archon:covers` but 58 lean-aux nodes (25+14+11+8) lack blueprint entries.

### Proofs lacking detail

- `Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` / headline theorem: only 1 `\leanok` across 8 declaration blocks; the main composition (`Milne Theorem 3.2`) has proof sketch but all subsidiary lemma bodies remain sorryed. The sketch names the two inputs (CodimOneExtension + AuslanderBuchsbaum) but does not detail how they compose into the full rational-map extension — insufficient for a prover to close without additional guidance.
- `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex`: the 9 `AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.*` stubs (representability engine, quot/valuative criteria) have prose stubs but no formalizable proof sketches — the prover has no blueprint path to close them.

### Lean difficulty quality

- `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` / `Picard_RelPicFunctor.tex`: 12 blueprint nodes in these chapters name Lean targets that do not exist at the current Lean tree:
  - `lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj` → `AlgebraicGeometry.LineBundle.OnProduct.pullback_tensorObj_iso` (wrong namespace; should be verified against `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`)
  - `lem:pullback_tensor_iso` → `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorIso` (does not exist)
  - `lem:pullback0_tensor_iso` → `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullback0TensorIso` (does not exist)
  - `lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv` → `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial` (does not exist)
  - `lem:isinvertible_implies_locallytrivial` → `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.IsInvertible.isLocallyTrivial` (does not exist)
  - `lem:isinvertible_pullback` → `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.IsInvertible.pullback` (does not exist)
  - `thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj` → `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (does not exist)
  - `lem:baseMap_pullbackComp_apply` / `lem:baseMap_pullback_comp_apply` / `lem:baseMap_pullbackCongr_apply` / `lem:baseMap_inv_step3_open_immersion` → 4 `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.*` names that do not exist in `Picard_QuotScheme.tex`
- `Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` / `lem:push_pull_functor` → `AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_comp` (does not exist; name mismatch with the actual `pushPullMap_comp` path).
- `Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex` / `thm:albanese_universal_property` → `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic.albaneseUP` (does not exist at current Lean tree; name refers to Pic.albaneseUP but the declaration lives elsewhere).
- `Jacobian.tex` / `thm:finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed` → `AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed` (does not exist).
- `Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex` / `lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula` → `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.ringKrullDim_localization_eq_relativeDimension` (does not exist — but `BareScheme.lean` defines `projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_X` which uses a related argument, so the name is likely stale/renamed).

### Multi-route coverage

- **Route A (PRIMARY)**: PASS — chapters exist and are ACTIVE for all phases A.1.c through A.4 and the genus-0 arm.
- **Route C (Riemann–Roch, PAUSED)**: all `RiemannRoch_*` chapters exist with inline sorries, treated as frozen per directive. Not flagged as must-fix. BUT: see critical disjointness finding below — Route-A cone pulls Route-C declarations.

### Dependency & isolation findings

**Route-A → Route-C disjointness violation (strategy-modifying):**
- `Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex` / `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (line 1297): `\uses{..., def:codim1_cycles, def:order_at_point, ...}` — both `def:codim1_cycles` and `def:order_at_point` are defined in `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` (Route-C, **PAUSED**). This places `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` — a declared `\leanok` declaration feeding the Milne Thm 3.2 chain — transitively on the paused route. **wire-up** direction: the `\uses{}` edges should either (a) be moved to an active-route chapter that re-defines the order-at-a-point and codim-1-cycles vocabulary, or (b) the declarations should be split out of `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` into a standalone chapter that both routes can use.
- `Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex` / `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (line 1472): `\uses{..., def:order_at_point, ...}` — same violation. Additionally carries `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.weilDivisorObstruction}` (unformalised stub).
- **Severity: STRATEGY-MODIFYING.** The active Route-A cone (`CodimOneExtension` is an A.4.a sub-row) has DAG edges into the permanently paused Route-C `WeilDivisor` chapter. A prover dispatched to `CodimOneExtension.lean` with objective `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` will reach `def:order_at_point` and find it in a paused chapter with a sorry body. The loop cannot close A.4.a without resolving this boundary — either by moving the Weil-divisor vocabulary to a shared active-route chapter, or by designating it explicitly as a sub-dependency of both routes with its own prover assignment.

**Known-issues cross-check:**
- `lem:S3_sep_2_*`, `lem:S3_pi_2_*`, `lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W`: isolation-EXEMPT per reviewer certification. Additionally, `lem:S3_sep_2_geom_reduced_finite_field_ext_is_separable` and `lem:S3_pi_2_isPurelyInseparable_of_unique_minPrime_baseChange` appear in the `unmatched_lean` list (their `\lean{}` targets don't exist in the Lean tree). These are already isolation-EXEMPT; no re-flag on isolation, but the `unmatched_lean` status means the Lean targets were never implemented. Consistent with descoped/superseded disposition.

**Unfaithful `\mathlibok` check:**
- `def:Abelian_Ext_chgUnivLinearEquiv` → `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv`: this node has a `\lean{}` hint pointing at a Mathlib declaration that does not exist. If this block carries `\mathlibok`, that would be an unfaithful anchor. Recommend verifying whether this block is `\mathlibok` or just a stub target. **Soft flag until chapter is confirmed** (the chapter could not be determined from leandag due to `unknown` chapter field).

---

## Unstarted-phase blueprint proposals

### Proposed chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_BareScheme.tex`

**Covers**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean`
**Strategy phase**: genusZero + witness body (gated A.3)

A dedicated chapter makes the substrate debt auditable and separates it from the large AbelianVarietyRigidity chapter.

**Key declarations** (dependency order):
1. `\definition` `\label{def:projectiveLineBarGrading}` — The standard ℕ-grading `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar` on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBarGrading}` [expected]. Source: standard graded algebra, no external ref needed.
